}\n616\t\n617\t    /// Mark the service as ready (all nodes reachable).\n618\t    pub async fn mark_ready(&self) {\n619\t        *self.ready.write().await = true;\n620\t        info!(\"Service marked as ready\");\n621\t    }\n622\t\n623\t    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n624\t    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n625\t        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n626\t        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n627\t\n628\t        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n629\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n630\t            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n631\t            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n632\t            if healthy.len() < required {\n633\t                return false;\n634\t            }\n635\t        }\n636\t\n637\t        true\n638\t    }\n639\t\n640\t    /// Sync rebalancer metrics to Prometheus (called from health checker).\n641\t    pub async fn sync_rebalancer_metrics_to_prometheus(&self) {\n642\t        if let Some(ref rebalancer) = self.rebalancer {\n643\t            let rebalancer_metrics = rebalancer.metrics.read().await;\n644\t            let in_progress = rebalancer_metrics.rebalance_start_time.is_some();\n645\t            self.metrics.set_rebalance_in_progress(in_progress);\n646\t\n647\t            // Calculate delta for documents migrated counter\n648\t            let current_total = rebalancer_metrics.documents_migrated_total;\n649\t            let previous = self.previous_docs_migrated.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n650\t            if current_total > previous {\n651\t                let delta = current_total - previous;\n652\t                self.metrics.inc_rebalance_documents_migrated(delta);\n653\t                self.previous_docs_migrated.store(current_total, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);\n654\t            }\n655\t\n656\t            let duration = rebalancer_metrics.current_duration_secs();\n657\t            if duration > 0.0 {\n658\t                self.metrics.observe_rebalance_duration(duration);\n659\t            }\n660\t        }\n661\t    }\n662\t}\n663\t\n664\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n665\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n666\tpub struct TopologyResponse {\n667\t    pub shards: u32,\n668\t    pub replication_factor: u32,\n669\t    pub nodes: Vec<NodeInfo>,\n670\t    pub degraded_node_count: u32,\n671\t    pub rebalance_in_progress: bool,\n672\t    pub fully_covered: bool,\n673\t}\n674\t\n675\t/// Per-node information in the topology response.\n676\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n677\tpub struct NodeInfo {\n678\t    pub id: String,\n679\t    pub address: String,\n680\t    pub status: String,\n681\t    pub shard_count: u32,\n682\t    pub last_seen_ms: u64,\n683\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n684\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n685\t}\n686\t\n687\t/// Response for GET /_miroir/shards.\n688\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n689\tpub struct ShardsResponse {\n690\t    pub shards: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>, // shard_id -> list of node IDs\n691\t}\n692\t\n693\t/// GET /_miroir/topology — full cluster state per plan §10.\n694\tpub async fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\n695\twhere\n696\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n697\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n698\t{\n699\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n700\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n701\t\n702\t    // Count degraded nodes\n703\t    let degraded_count = topo.nodes().filter(|n| !n.is_healthy()).count() as u32;\n704\t\n705\t    // Check rebalance status\n706\t    let rebalance_in_progress = if let Some(ref rebalancer) = state.rebalancer {\n707\t        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n708\t        status.in_progress\n709\t    } else {\n710\t        false\n711\t    };\n712\t\n713\t    // Build node info list\n714\t    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = topo\n715\t        .nodes()\n716\t        .map(|n| NodeInfo {\n717\t            id: n.id.as_str().to_string(),\n718\t            address: n.address.clone(),\n719\t            status: format!(\"{:?}\", n.status).to_lowercase(),\n720\t            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n721\t            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n722\t            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n723\t        })\n724\t        .collect();\n725\t\n726\t    // Check if fully covered\n727\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n728\t\n729\t    let response = TopologyResponse {\n730\t        shards: topo.shards,\n731\t        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n732\t        nodes,\n733\t        degraded_node_count: degraded_count,\n734\t        rebalance_in_progress,\n735\t        fully_covered,\n736\t    };\n737\t\n738\t    Ok(Json(response))\n739\t}\n740\t\n741\t/// GET /_miroir/shards — shard → node mapping table.\n742\tpub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\n743\twhere\n744\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n745\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n746\t{\n747\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n748\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n749\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n750\t\n751\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n752\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n753\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n754\t\n755\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n756\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n757\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n758\t            for node_id in assigned {\n759\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n760\t            }\n761\t        }\n762\t\n763\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n764\t    }\n765\t\n766\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n767\t}\n768\t\n769\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n770\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n771\twhere\n772\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n773\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n774\t{\n775\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n776\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n777\t\n778\t    if ready {\n779\t        Ok(\"\")\n780\t    } else {\n781\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n782\t        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n783\t        if has_quorum {\n784\t            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n785\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n786\t            Ok(\"\")\n787\t        } else {\n788\t            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n789\t        }\n790\t    }\n791\t}\n792\t\n793\t/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\n794\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\n795\twhere\n796\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n797\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n798\t{\n799\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n800\t    match state.metrics.encode_metrics() {\n801\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics.into_response(),\n802\t        Err(e) => {\n803\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n804\t            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n805\t        }\n806\t    }\n807\t}\n808\t\n809\t/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n810\t///\n811\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n812\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n813\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n814\t    State(state): State<S>,\n815\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n816\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n817\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n818\twhere\n819\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n820\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n821\t{\n822\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n823\t\n824\t    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n825\t        (\n826\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n827\t            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n828\t        )\n829\t    })?;\n830\t\n831\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n832\t        return Err((\n833\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n834\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n835\t        ));\n836\t    }\n837\t\n838\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n839\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n840\t        redis,\n841\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n842\t    };\n843\t\n844\t    info!(\n845\t        index = %index,\n846\t        force = body.force,\n847\t        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n848\t        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n849\t    );\n850\t\n851\t    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n852\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n853\t        Err(e) => {\n854\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n855\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n856\t        }\n857\t    }\n858\t}\n859\t\n860\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n861\tpub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n862\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n863\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n864\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n865\t    }\n866\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n867\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n868\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n869\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n870\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n871\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n872\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n873\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n874\t    };\n875\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n876\t}\n877\t\n878\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n879\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n880\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n881\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n882\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n883\t}\n884\t\n885\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n886\t///\n887\t/// Request body:\n888\t/// ```json\n889\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n890\t/// ```\n891\t///\n892\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n893\t/// ```json\n894\t/// { \"success\": true }\n895\t/// ```\n896\t///\n897\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n898\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n899\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n900\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n901\t///\n902\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n903\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n904\t    State(state): State<S>,\n905\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n906\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n907\t) -> Response\n908\twhere\n909\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n910\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n911\t{\n912\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n913\t\n914\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n915\t    let source_ip = headers\n916\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n917\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n918\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n919\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n920\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n921\t        .trim()\n922\t        .to_string();\n923\t\n924\t    // Parse rate limit config\n925\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n926\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n927\t        Err(e) => {\n928\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n929\t            return (\n930\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n931\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n932\t                    success: false,\n933\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n934\t                }),\n935\t            ).into_response();\n936\t        }\n937\t    };\n938\t\n939\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n940\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n941\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n942\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n943\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n944\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n945\t                    if !allowed {\n946\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n947\t                            warn!(\n948\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n949\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n950\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n951\t                            );\n952\t                            return (\n953\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n954\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n955\t                                    success: false,\n956\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n957\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n958\t                                        ws\n959\t                                    )),\n960\t                                }),\n961\t                            ).into_response();\n962\t                        } else {\n963\t                            return (\n964\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n965\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n966\t                                    success: false,\n967\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n968\t                                }),\n969\t                            ).into_response();\n970\t                        }\n971\t                    }\n972\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n973\t                }\n974\t                Err(e) => {\n975\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n976\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n977\t                }\n978\t            }\n979\t        }\n980\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n981\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n982\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n983\t            &source_ip,\n984\t            limit,\n985\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n986\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n987\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n988\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n989\t        );\n990\t        if !allowed {\n991\t            warn!(\n992\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n993\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n994\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n995\t            );\n996\t            return (\n997\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n998\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n999\t                    success: false,\n1000\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n1001\t                        Some(format!(\n1002\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n1003\t                            ws\n1004\t                        ))\n1005\t                    } else {\n1006\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n1007\t                    },\n1008\t                }),\n1009\t            ).into_response();\n1010\t        }\n1011\t    }\n1012\t\n1013\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n1014\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n1015\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n1016\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n1017\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1018\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1019\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n1020\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n1021\t                }\n1022\t            }\n1023\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1024\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n1025\t        }\n1026\t\n1027\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n1028\t        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n1029\t        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n1030\t            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n1031\t            Err(e) => {\n1032\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n1033\t                return (\n1034\t                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1035\t                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1036\t                        success: false,\n1037\t                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n1038\t                    }),\n1039\t                ).into_response();\n1040\t            }\n1041\t        };\n1042\t\n1043\t        info!(\n1044\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1045\t            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n1046\t            \"admin login successful\"\n1047\t        );\n1048\t\n1049\t        // Set cookie and return success\n1050\t        (\n1051\t            StatusCode::OK,\n1052\t            [\n1053\t                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n1054\t                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n1055\t            ],\n1056\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1057\t                success: true,\n1058\t                message: None,\n1059\t            }),\n1060\t        ).into_response()\n1061\t    } else {\n1062\t        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n1063\t        warn!(\n1064\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n1065\t            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n1066\t        );\n1067\t\n1068\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n1069\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n1070\t                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1071\t                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1072\t                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1073\t\n1074\t                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n1075\t                    &source_ip,\n1076\t                    failed_threshold,\n1077\t                    backoff_start_minutes,\n1078\t                    backoff_max_hours,\n1079\t                ) {\n1080\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n1081\t                }\n1082\t            }\n1083\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n1084\t            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n1085\t            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n1086\t            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n1087\t\n1088\t            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n1089\t                &source_ip,\n1090\t                failed_threshold,\n1091\t                backoff_start_minutes,\n1092\t                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n1093\t            );\n1094\t        }\n1095\t\n1096\t        (\n1097\t            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n1098\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n1099\t                success: false,\n1100\t                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n1101\t            }),\n1102\t        ).into_response()\n1103\t    }\n1104\t}\n1105\t\n1106\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1107\t// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n1108\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n1109\t\n1110\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n1111\t///\n1112\t/// Request body:\n1113\t/// ```json\n1114\t/// {\n1115\t///   \"id\": \"node-new\",\n1116\t///   \"address\": \"http://node-new:7700\",\n1117\t///   \"replica_group\": 0\n1118\t/// }\n1119\t/// ```\n1120\t///\n1121\t/// Implements plan §2 \"Adding a node to an existing group\":\n1122\t/// 1. Add node to topology in `Joining` state\n1123\t/// 2. Send `NodeAdded` event to rebalancer worker\n1124\t/// 3. Worker computes affected shards and starts migration with leader lease\n1125\tpub async fn add_node<S>(\n1126\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1127\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1128\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1129\twhere\n1130\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1131\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1132\t{\n1133\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1134\t\n1135\t    let id = body.get(\"id\")\n1136\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1137\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'id' field\".into()))?\n1138\t        .to_string();\n1139\t\n1140\t    let address = body.get(\"address\")\n1141\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n1142\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'address' field\".into()))?\n1143\t        .to_string();\n1144\t\n1145\t    let replica_group = body.get(\"replica_group\")\n1146\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n1147\t        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'replica_group' field\".into()))?\n1148\t        as u32;\n1149\t\n1150\t    // Add node to topology\n1151\t    {\n1152\t        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n1153\t        // Check if node already exists\n1154\t        let node_id = NodeId::new(id.clone());\n1155\t        if topo.node(&node_id).is_some() {\n1156\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1157\t                format!(\"Node {} already exists\", id)));\n1158\t        }\n1159\t        // Check if replica group exists\n1160\t        let group_count = topo.groups().count() as u32;\n1161\t        if replica_group >= group_count {\n1162\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST,\n1163\t                format!(\"Replica group {} does not exist\", replica_group)));\n1164\t        }\n1165\t        let node = Node::new(node_id, address, replica_group);\n1166\t        topo.add_node(node);\n1167\t    }\n1168\t\n1169\t    // Send event to rebalancer worker (if available)\n1170\t    if let Some(ref worker) = app_state.rebalancer_worker {\n1171\t        let event = TopologyChangeEvent::NodeAdded {\n1172\t            node_id: id.clone(),\n1173\t            replica_group,\n1174\t            index_uid: \"default\".to_string(),\n1175\t        };\n1176\t        if let Err(e) = worker.event_sender().try_send(event) {\n1177\t            error!(error = %e, node_id = %id, \"failed to send NodeAdded event to rebalancer worker\");\n1178\t            return Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n1179\t                format!(\"Failed to queue rebalancing: {}\", e)));\n1180\t        }\n1181\t    }\n1182\t\n1183\t    info!(node_id = %id, replica_group, \"Node addition queued for rebalancing\");\n1184\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n1185\t        \"node_id\": id,\n1186\t        \"replica_group\": replica_group,\n1187\t        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} added to replica group {}, rebalancing will start shortly\", id, replica_group),\n1188\t    })))\n1189\t}\n1190\t\n1191\t/// DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id} — Remove a node from the cluster.\n1192\t///\n1193\t/// Request body (optional):\n1194\t/// ```json\n1195\t/// {\n1196\t///   \"force\": false  // Set to true to bypass draining check\n1197\t/// }\n1198\t/// ```\n1199\t///\n1200\t/// Requires the node to be in `draining` state unless `force=true`.\n1201\t/// Note: This only removes the node from topology. Draining must be completed first.\n1202\tpub async fn remove_node<S>(\n1203\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1204\t    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n1205\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1206\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1207\twhere\n1208\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1209\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1210\t{\n1211\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n1212\t\n1213\t    let force = body.get(\"force\")\n1214\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n1215\t        .unwrap_or(false);\n1216\t\n1217\t    let node_id_obj = NodeId::new(node_id.clone());\n1218\t\n1219\t    // Check node state\n1220\t    let node_status = {\n1221\t        let topo = app_state.topology.read().await;\n1222\t        let node = topo.node(&node_id_obj)\n1223\t            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, format!(\"Node {} not found\", node_id)))?;\n1224\t\n1225\t        // Check if this is the last node in the group\n1226\t        let group = topo.groups()\n1227\t            .find(|g| g.id == node.replica_group)\n1228\t            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!(\"Replica group {} not found\", node.replica_group)))?;\n1229\t\n1230\t        if group.nodes().len() <= 1 {\n1231\t            return Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Cannot remove the last node in a replica group\".into()));\n1232\t        }\n1233\t\n1234\t        node.status\n1235\t    };\n1236\t\n1237\t    if !force && node_status != miroir_core::topology::NodeStatus::Draining {\n1238\t        return Err((StatusCode::CONFLICT, format!(\n1239\t            \"Node {} is not in draining state (current: {:?}). Drain the node first with POST /_miroir/nodes/{}/drain, or use force=true to bypass\",\n1240\t            node_id, node_status, node_id\n1241\t        ).into()));\n1242\t    }\n1243\t\n1244\t    // Remove node from topology\n1245\t    {\n1246\t        let mut topo = app_state.topology.write().await;\n1247\t        topo.remove_node(&node_id_obj);\n1248\t    }\n1249\t\n1250\t    info!(node_id = %node_id, force, \"Node removal completed\");\n1251\t    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n1252\t        \"node_id\": node_id,\n1253\t        \"message\": format!(\"Node {} removed from cluster\", node_id),\n1254\t    })))\n1255\t}\n1256\t\n1257\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain — Drain a node (prepare for removal).\n1258\t///\n1259\t/// Request body (optional):\n1260\t/// ```json\n1261\t/// {\n1262\t///   \"force\": false  // Set to true to bypass safety checks\n1263\t/// }\n1264\t/// ```\n1265\t///\n1266\t/// Implements plan §2 node drain flow:\n1267\t/// 1. Mark node as `draining`\n1268\t/// 2. Send `NodeDraining` event to rebalancer worker\n1269\t/// 3. Worker computes shard destinations and starts migration with leader lease\n1270\t///\n1271\t/// Safety check: refuses drain if it would drop a shard below RF=1 in its group\n1272\t/// AND the group has no healthy peer group to fall back to. Use --force to override.\n1273\tpub async fn drain_node<S>(\n1274\t    State(state): State<S>,\n1275\t    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n1276\t    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n1277\t) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\n1278\twhere\n1279\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n1280\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n1281\t{\n1282\t    let app_state = (replica_group, warning)\n1348\t    };\n1349\t\n1350\t    // Send event to rebalancer worker\n1351\t    let event = TopologyChangeEvent::NodeDraining {\n1352\t        node_id: node_id.clone(),\n1353\t Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\n4\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n5\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n6\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n7\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n8\t\n9\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n10\t///\n11\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n12\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n13\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n14\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n15\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n16\t    h.finish()\n17\t}\n18\t\n19\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n20\t///\n21\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n24\t/// for deterministic resolution.\n25\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n26\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n27\t        .iter()\n28\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n29\t        .collect();\n30\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n31\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n32\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n33\t    });\n34\t    scored\n35\t        .into_iter()\n36\t        .take(rf)\n37\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n38\t        .collect()\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n42\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n43\t    topology\n44\t        .groups()\n45\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n46\t        .collect()\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n50\t///\n51\t/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;\n101\t    }\n102\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n106\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n107\t    (0..shard_count)\n108\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n109\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n110\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n111\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n112\t        })\n113\t        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n114\t        .into_iter()\n115\t        .collect()\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n119\t///\n120\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n121\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n122\tpub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n123\t    shard_count: u32,\n124\t    group: &Group,\n125\t    rf: usize,\n126\t    query_seq: u64,\n127\t    index: &str,\n128\t    floor: u64,\n129\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n130\t) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n131\t    let mut result = Vec::new();\n132\t    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n133\t\n134\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n136\t\n137\t        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n138\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n139\t            .iter()\n140\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n141\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n142\t                version >= floor\n143\t            })\n144\t            .collect();\n145\t\n146\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n147\t            // No eligible replica for this shard\n148\t            return None;\n149\t        }\n150\t\n151\t        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n152\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n153\t        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n154\t            result.push(selected.clone());\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    Some(result)\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n162\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n163\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n164\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n165\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n169\t///\n170\t/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n171\t/// For each shard, {\n176\t    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n177\t        .iter()\n178\t        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n179\t        .collect();\n180\t\n181\t    let change once)\n185\t            for node in new_nodes {\n186\t                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n187\t                    diff += 1;\n188\t                }\n189\t            }\n190\t        }\n191\t    }\n192\t    diff\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t#[cfg(test)]\n196\tmod tests {\n197\t    use super::*;\n198\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n199\t    use  fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n420\t        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n421\t        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n422\t\n423\t        // Different shard_id → different score\n424\t        let score_0_a = score(0, node_a);\n425\t        let score_1_a = score(1, node_a);\n426\t        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_1_a, \"Same node, different shard should differ\");\n427\t\n428\t        // }\n506\t\n507\t    /// P1.3-A3: covering_set covers all shards within the chosen group.\n508\t    #[test]\n509\t    fn test_covering_set_covers_all_shards() {\n510\t        let mut topo = outputs.\n561\t    #[test]\n562\t    fn test_covering_set_determinism() {\n563\t        let mut topo1 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n564\t        let mut topo2 = Topology::new(64, 2, 2);\n565\t\n566\t std::collections::HashSet<_> = c1.into_iter().collect();\n585\t            let s2: std::collections::HashSet<_> = c2.into_iter().collect();\n586\t            assert_eq!(\n587\t                s1, s2,\n588\t                \"covering_set differs for identical topologies at query_seq={}\",\n589\t                query_seq\n590\t            );\n591\t        }\n592\t    }\n593\t\n594\t    /// P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n595\t    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n597\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n598\t        let samples = 10_000;\n599\t\n600\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n601\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n602\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n603\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n604\t        }\n605\t\n606\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n607\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n608\t\n609\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n610\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n611\t            .iter()\n612\t            .map(|&observed| {\n613\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n614\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n615\t            })\n616\t            .sum();\n617\t\n618\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n619\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n620\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n621\t\n622\t        assert!(\n623\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n624\t            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n625\t            chi_square,\n626\t            critical_value\n627\t        );\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set Topology management: node registry, groups, health state, and state machine.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n4\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Deserializer, Serialize, Serializer};\n5\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n6\tuse std::time::Instant;\n7\t\n8\t/// Unique identifier for a node.\n9\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n10\tpub struct NodeId(String);\n11\t\n12\timpl NodeId {\n13\t    pub fn new(id: String) -> Self {\n14\t        Self(id)\n15\t    }\n16\t\n17\t    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n18\t        &self.0\n19\t    }\n20\t}\n21\t\n22\timpl From<String> for NodeId {\n23\t    fn from(s: String) -> Self {\n24\t        Self(s)\n25\t    }\n26\t}\n27\t\n28\timpl AsRef<str> for NodeId {\n29\t    fn as_ref(&self) -> &str {\n30\t        &self.0\n31\t    }\n32\t}\n33\t\n34\timpl std::fmt::Display for NodeId {\n35\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n36\t        self.0.fmt(f)\n37\t    }\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t/// Health status of a node, with state-machine transitions.\n41\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n42\t#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\n43\tpub enum NodeStatus {\n44\t    /// Node is healthy and serving traffic.\n45\t    #[default]\n46\t    Healthy,\n47\t    /// Node is degraded (timeouts, not full disconnect) but still serving.\n48\t    Degraded,\n49\t    /// Node is draining — graceful shutdown, still serves shards it owns.\n50\t    Draining,\n51\t    /// Node has failed (unplanned outage).\n52\t    Failed,\n53\t    /// Node is joining the cluster (being provisioned).\n54\t    Joining,\n55\t    /// Node is active — fully operational after joining.\n56\t    Active,\n57\t    /// Node has been removed from the cluster.\n58\t    Removed,\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\timpl NodeStatus {\n62\t    /// Attempt a state transition. Returns the new status on success.\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// Legal transitions (plan §2 topology-change verbs):\n65\t    /// - (new) → Joining          (admin API: POST /_miroir/nodes)\n66\t    /// - Joining → Active         (migration complete)\n67\t    /// - Active → Draining        (admin API: POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain)\n68\t    /// - Draining → Removed       (migration complete)\n69\t    /// - Active/Draining → Failed (health check detects failure)\n70\t    /// - Failed → Active          (health check recovery)\n71\t    /// - Active/Failed → Degraded (partial health: timeouts)\n72\t    /// - Degraded → Active        (health restored)\n73\t    pub fn transition_to(self, target: NodeStatus) -> Result<NodeStatus> {\n74\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n75\t\n76\t        let legal = match (self, target) {\n77\t            // Normal lifecycle\n78\t            (Joining, Active) => true,\n79\t            (Active, Draining) => true,\n80\t            (Draining, Removed) => true,\n81\t\n82\t            // Failure detection\n83\t            (Active, Failed) => true,\n84\t            (Draining, Failed) => true,\n85\t\n86\t            // Recovery\n87\t            (Failed, Active) => true,\n88\t\n89\t            // Degraded\n90\t            (Active, Degraded) => true,\n91\t            (Failed, Degraded) => true,\n92\t            (Degraded, Active) => true,\n93\t\n94\t            // Idempotent\n95\t            (Active, Active)\n96\t            | (Failed, Failed)\n97\t            | (Degraded, Degraded)\n98\t            | (Joining, Joining)\n99\t            | (Draining, Draining) => true,\n100\t\n101\t            // Healthy is an alias for Active in transitions\n102\t            (Healthy, _) | (_, Healthy) => false,\n103\t\n104\t            _ => false,\n105\t        };\n106\t\n107\t        if legal {\n108\t            Ok(target)\n109\t        } else {\n110\t            Err(MiroirError::Topology(format!(\n111\t                \"illegal state transition: {:?} → {:?}\",\n112\t                self, target\n113\t            )))\n114\t        }\n115\t    }\n116\t\n117\t    /// Check if a node in this status is serving reads.\n118\t    pub fn is_readable(self) -> bool {\n119\t        matches!(\n120\t            self,\n121\t            NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded | NodeStatus::Draining\n122\t        )\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    /// Check if a node in this status can accept any writes unconditionally.\n126\t    pub fn is_active(self) -> bool {\n127\t        matches!(self, NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded)\n128\t    }\n129\t}\n130\t\n131\t/// A single Meilisearch node in the topology.\n132\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n133\tpub struct Node {\n134\t    /// Unique node identifier.\n135\t    pub id: NodeId,\n136\t\n137\t    /// Node base URL (e.g., \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\").\n138\t    pub address: String,\n139\t\n140\t    /// Instant of the last successful health check.\n148\t    #[serde(skip)]\n149\t    pub last_seen: Option<Instant>,\n150\t\n151\t    /// Error message from the last failed health check.\n152\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n153\t    pub last_error: Option<String>,\n154\t}\n155\t\n156\timpl Node {\n157\t    /// Create a new node (starts in Joining state).\n158\t    pub fn new(id: NodeId, address: String, replica_group: u32) -> Self {\n159\t        Self {\n160\t            id,\n161\t            address,\n162\t            status: NodeStatus::Joining,\n163\t            replica_group,\n164\t            last_seen: None,\n165\t            last_error: None,\n166\t        }\n167\t    }\n168\t\n169\t    /// Check if this node can receive writes for a shard.\n170\t    ///\n171\t    /// `shard_affected` is true when the shard is being migrated away from this\n172\t    /// node during a drain. Draining nodes still accept writes for shards they\n173\t    /// still own (`shard_affected = false`).\n174\t    pub fn is_write_eligible_for(&self, shard_affected: bool) -> bool {\n175\t        match self.status {\n176\t            NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded => true,\n177\t            NodeStatus::Draining => !shard_affected,\n178\t            NodeStatus::Joining | NodeStatus::Failed | NodeStatus::Removed => false,\n179\t        }\n180\t    }\n181\t\n182\t    /// Check if the node is healthy (can serve traffic).\n183\t    pub fn is_healthy(&self) -> bool {\n184\t        matches!(\n185\t            self.status,\n186\t            NodeStatus::Active | NodeStatus::Healthy | NodeStatus::Degraded\n187\t        )\n188\t    }\n189\t\n190\t    /// Attempt a state transition on this node.\n191\t    pub fn transition_to(&mut self, target: NodeStatus) -> Result<()> {\n192\t        self.status = self.status.transition_to(target)?;\n193\t        Ok(())\n194\t    }\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\t/// A replica group: an independent query pool.\n198\t///\n199\t/// Each group holds all S shards, distributed across its nodes.\n200\t/// Reads are routed to a single group per query.\n201\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n202\tpub struct Group {\n203\t    /// Group identifier add_node(&mut self, node_id: NodeId) {\n221\t        if !self.nodes.contains(&node_id) {\n222\t            self.nodes.push(node_id);\n223\t        }\n224\t    }\n225\t\n226\t    /// Get the node IDs in this group.\n227\t    pub fn nodes(&self) -> &[NodeId] {\n228\t        &self.nodes\n229\t    }\n230\t\n231\t    /// Get the number of nodes in this group.\n232\t    pub fn node_count(&self) ->     .filter_map(|id| node_map.get(id))\n243\t            .filter(|n| n.is_healthy())\n244\t            .collect()\n245\t    }\n246\t}\n247\t\n248\t/// Cluster topology: groups, nodes, and health state.\n249\t///\n250\t/// Serializes/deserializes from plan §4 YAML format:\n251\t/// ```yaml\n252\t/// shards: 64\n253\t/// replica_groups: 2\n254\t/// rf: 1\n255\t/// nodes:\n256\t///   - id: \"meili-0\"\n257\t///     address: \"http://meili-0:7700\"\n258\t///     replica_group: 0\n259\t/// ```\n260\t///\n261\t/// Groups are derived from node `replica_group` fields and rebuilt automatically.\n262\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n263\tpub struct Topology {\n264\t    /// Total number of logical shards.\n265\t    pub shards: u32,\n266\t\n267\t    /// Number of replica groups.\n268\t    pub replica_groups: u32,\n269\t\n270\t    /// Replication factor (intra-group).\n271\t    pub rf: usize,\n272\t\n273\t    /// All nodes in the cluster.\n274\t    pub nodes: Vec<Node>,\n275\t\n276\t    /// Derived group index (rebuilt from nodes).\n277\t    groups: Vec<Group>,\n278\t\n279\t    /// Node ID → Vec index lookup.\n280\t    node_index: HashMap<NodeId, usize>,\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\timpl Topology {\n284\t    /// Create a new empty topology.\n285\t    pub fn new(shards: u32, replica_groups: u32, rf: usize) -> Self {\n286\t        Self {\n287\t            shards,\n288\t            replica_groups,\n289\t            rf,\n290\t            nodes: Vec::new(),\n291\t            groups: (0..replica_groups).map(Group::new).collect(),\n292\t            node_index: HashMap::new(),\n293\t        }\n294\t    }\n295\t\n296\t    /// Add a node to the topology.\n297\t    pub fn add_node(&mut self, node: Node) {\n298\t        let idx = self.nodes.len();\n299\t        self.node_index.insert(node.id.clone(), idx);\n300\t        let group_id = node.replica_group;\n301\t        self.nodes.push(node);\n302\t        self.rebuild_groups();\n303\t        self.replica_groups = self.replica_groups.max(group_id + 1);\n304\t    }\n305\t\n306\t    /// Get a node by ID.\n307\t    pub fn node(&self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&Node> {\n308\t        self.node_index.get(id).map(|&i| &self.nodes[i])\n309\t    }\n310\t\n311\t    /// Get a mutable node by ID.\n312\t    pub fn node_mut(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> Option<&mut Node> {\n313\t        self.node_index.get(id).copied().map(move |i| &mut self.nodes[i])\n314\t    }\n315\t\n316\t    /// Iterate over all nodes.\n317\t    pub fn nodes(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Node> {\n318\t        self.nodes.iter()\n319\t    }\n320\t\n321\t    /// Get a group by ID.\n322\t    pub fn group(&self, id: u32) -> Option<&Group> {\n323\t        self.groups.get(id as usize)\n324\t    }\n325\t\n326\t    /// Iterate over all groups in ascending order by ID.\n327\t    pub fn groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = &Group> {\n328\t        self.groups.iter()\n329\t    }\n330\t\n331\t    /// Get the {\n338\t        self.groups.len() as u32\n339\t    }\n340\t\n341\t    /// Build a HashMap<NodeId, Node> for use with Group::healthy_nodes.\n342\t    pub fn node_map(&self) -> HashMap<NodeId, Node> {\n343\t        self.nodes.iter().map(|n| (n.id.clone(), n.clone())).collect()\n344\t    }\n345\t\n346\t    /// Remove a node from the topology.\n347\t    pub fn remove_node(&mut self, id: &NodeId) -> bool {\n348\t        if let Some(&idx) = self.node_index.get(id) {\n349\t            self.node_index.remove(id);\n350\t            self.nodes.remove(idx);\n351\t            // Rebuild indices\n352\t            self.node_index.clear();\n353\t            for (i, node) in self.nodes.iter().enumerate() {\n354\t                self.node_index.insert(node.id.clone(), i);\n355\t            }\n356\t            self.rebuild_groups();\n357\t            true\n358\t        } else {\n359\t            false\n360\t        }\n361\t    }\n362\t\n363\t    /// Remove all nodes in a replica group and the group itself.\n364\t    pub fn remove_group(&mut self, group_id: u32) -> bool {\n365\t        // Find all nodes in this group\n366\t        let nodes_to_remove: Vec<NodeId> = self\n367\t            .nodes\n368\t            .iter()\n369\t            .filter(|n| n.replica_group == group_id)\n370\t            .map(|n| n.id.clone())\n371\t            .collect();\n372\t\n373\t        if nodes_to_remove.is_empty() {\n374\t            return false;\n375\t        }\n376\t\n377\t        // Remove all nodes in the group\n378\t        for node_id in nodes_to_remove {\n379\t            self.remove_node(&node_id);\n380\t        }\n381\t\n382\t        true\n383\t    }\n384\t\n385\t    fn rebuild_groups(&mut self) {\n386\t        let num_groups = self\n387\t            .nodes\n388\t            .iter()\n389\t            .map(|n| n.replica_group)\n390\t            .max()\n391\t            .map_or(self.replica_groups as usize, |m| (m as usize + 1).max(self.replica_groups as usize));\n392\t\n393\t        self.groups = (0..num_groups).map(|i| Group::new(i as u32)).collect();\n394\t        for node in &self.nodes {\n395\t            if let Some(group) = self.groups.get_mut(node.replica_group as usize) {\n396\t                group.add_node(node.id.clone());\n397\t            }\n398\t        }\n399\t    }\n400\t}\n401\t\n402\timpl Serialize for Topology {\n403\t    fn serialize<S: Serializer>(&self, serializer: S) -> std::result::Result<S::Ok, S::Error> {\n404\t        #[derive(Serialize)]\n405\t        struct TopologyData {\n406\t            shards: u32,\n407\t            replica_groups: u32,\n408\t            rf: usize,\n409\t            nodes: Vec<Node>,\n410\t        }\n411\t        TopologyData {\n412\t            shards: self.shards,\n413\t            replica_groups: self.replica_groups,\n414\t            rf: self.rf,\n415\t            nodes: self.nodes.clone(),\n416\t        }\n417\t        .serialize(serializer)\n418\t    }\n419\t}\n420\t\n421\timpl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for Topology {\n422\t    fn deserialize<D: Deserializer<'de>>(deserializer: D) -> std::result::Result<Self, D::Error> {\n423\t        #[derive(Deserialize)]\n424\t        struct TopologyData {\n425\t            shards: u32,\n426\t            #[serde(default)]\n427\t            replica_groups: u32,\n428\t            #[serde(alias = \"replication_factor\", default)]\n429\t            rf: usize,\n430\t            #[serde(default)]\n431\t            nodes: Vec<Node>,\n432\t        }\n433\t\n434\t        let data = TopologyData::deserialize(deserializer)?;\n435\t        let mut topo = Self {\n436\t            shards: data.shards,\n437\t            replica_groups: data.replica_groups,\n438\t            rf: data.rf,\n439\t            nodes: data.nodes,\n440\t            groups: Vec::new(),\n441\t            node_index: HashMap::new(),\n442\t        };\n443\t        // Build lookup index\n444\t        for (i, node) in topo.nodes.iter().enumerate() {\n445\t            topo.node_index.insert(node.id.clone(), i);\n446\t        }\n447\t        // Derive replica_groups from nodes if not set\n448\t        if topo.replica_groups == 0 && !topo.nodes.is_empty() {\n449\t            topo.replica_groups = topo.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.replica_group).max().unwrap() + 1;\n450\t        }\n451\t        topo.rebuild_groups();\n452\t        Ok(topo)\n453\t    }\n454\t}\n455\t\n456\t#[cfg(test)]\n457\tmod tests {\n458\t    use super::*;\n459\t\n460\t    // ── YAML deserialization {\n576\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n577\t\n578\t        let cases: Vec<(NodeStatus, NodeStatus)> = vec![\n579\t            (Joining, Active),\n580\t            (Active, Draining),\n581\t            (Draining, Removed),\n582\t            (Active, Failed),\n583\t            (Draining, Failed),\n584\t            (Failed, Active),\n585\t            (Active, Degraded),\n586\t            (Failed, Degraded),\n587\t            (Degraded, Active),\n588\t            // Idempotent\n589\t            (Active, Active),\n590\t            (Failed, Failed),\n591\t            (Degraded, Degraded),\n592\t            (Joining, Joining),\n593\t            (Draining, Draining),\n594\t        ];\n595\t\n596\t        for (from, to) in cases {\n597\t            assert!(\n598\t                from.transition_to(to).is_ok(),\n599\t                \"expected {:?} → {:?} to succeed\",\n600\t                from,\n601\t                to\n602\t            );\n603\t        }\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    #[test]\n607\t    fn illegal_transitions() {\n608\t        use NodeStatus::*;\n609\t\n610\t        let cases: Vec<(NodeStatus, NodeStatus)> = vec![\n611\t            // Skip steps\n612\t            (Joining, Draining),\n613\t            (Joining, Failed),\n614\t            (Joining, Removed),\n615\t            (Joining, Degraded),\n616\t            // Can't go back from Draining to Active\n617\t            (Draining, Active),\n618\t            (Draining, Joining),\n619\t            // Can't recover to Joining\n620\t            (Active, Joining),\n621\t            (Failed, Joining),\n622\t            (Degraded, Joining),\n623\t            // Removed is terminal\n624\t            (Removed, Active),\n625\t            (Removed, Joining),\n626\t            (Removed, Failed),\n627\t            (Removed, Degraded),\n628\t            (Removed, Draining),\n629\t            // Healthy not used in transitions\n630\t            (Healthy, Active),\n631\t            (Active, Healthy),\n632\t            // More illegal paths\n633\t            (Failed, Draining),\n634\t            (Failed, Removed),\n635\t            (Degraded, Failed),\n636\t            (Degraded, Draining),\n637\t            (Degraded, Removed),\n638\t        ];\n639\t\n640\t        for (from, to) in cases {\n641\t            let result = from.transition_to(to);\n642\t            assert!(\n643\t                result.is_err(),\n644\t                \"expected {:?} → {:?} to be rejected, but got Ok({:?})\",\n645\t                from,\n646\t                to,\n647\t                result.unwrap()\n648\t            );\n649\t        }\n650\t    }\n651\t\n652\t    #[test]\n653\t    fn node_transition_method() {\n654\t        let mut node = Node::new(\n655\t            NodeId::new(\"n0\".into()),\n656\t            \"http://n0:7700\".into(),\n657\t            0,\n658\t        );\n659\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Joining);\n660\t\n661\t        node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active).unwrap();\n662\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active);\n663\t\n664\t        // Illegal: Active → Joining\n665\t        assert!(node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Joining).is_err());\n666\t        assert_eq!(node.status, NodeStatus::Active); // unchanged\n667\t    }\n668\t\n669\t    #[test]\n670\t    fn {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_e72a2119c14349cbae41fa3f","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Shard migration cutover state machine.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements the node-addition migration flow from plan §4 with explicit state\n4\t//! transitions and a race-window-safe cutover sequence.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! ## Race window analysis (plan §15 OP#1)\n7\t//!\n8\t//! The dangerous window is between \"mark node active\" (routing changes to new-node-only)\n9\t//! and \"delete migrated shard from old node.\" A document written during dual-write that\n10\t//! succeeded on OLD but failed on NEW — and arrived after the last migration page —\n11\t//! would be deleted from OLD without ever reaching NEW.\n12\t//!\n13\t//! ## Solution: quiesce-then-verify cutover\n14\t//!\n15\t//! Instead of the naïve sequence (mark active → stop dual-write → delete old), we use:\n16\t//!\n17\t//! 1. Stop dual-write (no new writes go to either node for affected shards)\n18\t//! 2. Drain: wait for all in-flight writes to both OLD and NEW to complete\n19\t//! 3. Delta migration: re-read affected shards from OLD (catches anything written since\n20\t//!    the last migration page) and write deltas to NEW\n21\t//! 4. Mark node active (routing switches to NEW-only)\n22\t//! 5. Delete migrated shard from OLD\n23\t//!\n24\t//! Step 3 is the key: it closes the race window by ensuring NEW has a complete picture\n25\t//! before we commit the routing change. The cost is one extra pagination pass over each\n26\t//! migrated shard — bounded by the number of docs written during the migration window.\n27\t\n28\tuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\n29\tuse std::fmt;\n30\tuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n31\t\n32\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n33\t\n34\t/// Unique identifier for a shard migration PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n69\tpub enum ShardMigrationState {\n70\t    /// Waiting for background migration to begin.\n71\t    Pending,\n72\t    /// Background pagination is reading docs from source and writing to target.\n73\t    Migrating {\n74\t        docs_copied: u64,\n75\t        pages_remaining: u32,\n76\t    },\n77\t    /// Background migration complete, awaiting cutover.\n78\t    MigrationComplete { docs_copied: u64 },\n79\t    /// Dual-write stopped, in-flight writes draining.\n80\t    Draining {\n81\t        in_flight_count: u32,\n82\t        docs_copied:    },\n89\t    /// Node is active for this shard; old replica data deleted.\n90\t    Active,\n91\t    /// Migration failed at this phase.\n92\t    Failed { phase: String, reason: String             docs_copied,\n114\t            } => {\n115\t                write!(\n116\t                    f,\n117\t                    \"draining({in_flight_count} in-flight, {docs_copied} Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the miroir codebase. Let me compile my findings into a detailed report.\n\n## Exploration Report: Miroir Codebase Architecture\n\nBased on my exploration of the miroir codebase, here's a comprehensive analysis of the key components:\n\n### 1. Key File Paths and Their Purposes\n\n**Core Node Management & Topology:**\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/topology.rs` - Core topology management, node registry, health state machine\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs` - Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/scatter.rs` - Scatter orchestration and fan-out logic\n\n**Rebalancer & Background Workers:**\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/rebalancer.rs` - Main rebalancer implementation with topology operations\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/rebalancer_worker/mod.rs` - Background worker with advisory lock\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/migration.rs` - Shard migration state machine and dual-write logic\n\n**Admin API:**\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/admin.rs` - Admin router definition\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/admin_endpoints.rs` - Admin endpoint implementations\n\n**Configuration:**\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/config.rs` - Main configuration structure\n- `/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/config/advanced.rs` - Advanced configuration options\n\n### 2. Current Node State Representation\n\n**Node Structure** (`topology.rs`):\n```rust\npub struct Node {\n    pub id: NodeId,              // Unique node identifier\n    pub address: String,         // Node base URL (e.g., \"http://meili-0:7700\")\n    pub status: NodeStatus,      // Current health status\n    pub replica_group: u32,      // Replica group assignment (0-based)\n    pub last_seen: Option<Instant>,  // Last successful health check\n    pub last_error: Option<String>,   // Last health check error\n}\n```\n\n**Node Status States** (`topology.rs`):\n```rust\npub enum NodeStatus {\n    Healthy,   // Node is healthy and serving traffic\n    Degraded,  // Node is degraded (timeouts) but still serving\n    Draining,  // Node is draining — graceful shutdown\n    Failed,    // Node has failed (unplanned outage)\n    Joining,   // Node is joining the cluster\n    Active,    // Node is active — fully operational\n    Removed,   // Node has been removed from the cluster\n}\n```\n\nThe system includes a comprehensive state machine with legal transitions and safety checks.\n\n### 3. How Assignments Are Currently Computed\n\n**Shard Assignment** (`router.rs`):\n- Uses **Rendezvous hashing** for deterministic shard-to-node assignment\n- `assign_shard_in_group()` assigns shards to RF nodes within a replica group\n- `score()` function computes hash-based scores for shard+node pairs\n- Higher scores win; ties broken lexicographically by node_id\n\n**Write Targets** (`router.rs`):\n- `write_targets()` returns all write targets: RF nodes in EACH replica group\n- `write_targets_with_migration()` includes dual-write targets during migration\n- During dual-write phase, writes go to both old and new nodes for safety\n\n**Query Routing** (`scatter.rs`):\n- `plan_search_scatter()` creates scatter plans for search queries\n- `query_group()` selects replica group using round-robin by query counter\n- `covering_set()` ensures one node per shard within chosen group\n\n### 4. Where the Rebalancer/Background Worker Lives\n\n**Main Rebalancer** (`rebalancer.rs`):\n- `Rebalancer` struct orchestrates topology changes\n- Methods: `add_node()`, `drain_node()`, `remove_node()`, `handle_node_failure()`\n- Runs background worker via `run_background()` method\n- Uses leader election via `TaskStore` for distributed coordination\n\n**Background Worker** (`rebalancer_worker/mod.rs`):\n- `RebalancerWorker` implements advisory lock pattern\n- Reacts to `TopologyChangeEvent` enum (NodeAdded, NodeDraining, NodeFailed, NodeRecovered)\n- Maintains per-shard migration state with progress tracking\n- Uses `TaskStore` for job persistence and resumability\n\n**Additional Workers**:\n- `DriftReconciler` - Settings drift reconciliation\n- `AntiEntropyWorker` - Continuous data reconciliation\n- `ModeCWorker` - Chunked background jobs\n\n### 5. Existing Patterns for Async Background Tasks\n\n**Dual-Write Pattern** (`migration.rs`):\n```rust\npub enum ShardMigrationState {\n    Pending,\n    Migrating { docs_copied: u64, pages_remaining: u32 },\n    MigrationComplete { docs_copied: u64 },\n    Draining { in_flight_count: u32, docs_copied: u64 },\n    DeltaPass { docs_copied: u64, delta_docs_copied: u64 },\n    Active,\n    Failed { phase: String, reason: String },\n}\n```\n\n**Migration Phases** (`migration.rs`):\n1. `ComputingAssignments` - Computing which shards move\n2. `DualWriteMigrating` - Dual-write active; background migration\n3. `CutoverBegin` - Background migration done\n4. `CutoverDraining` - Stopping dual-write; waiting for in-flight writes\n5. `CutoverDeltaPass` - Re-reading source to catch stragglers\n6. `CutoverActivate` - Marking new node active\n7. `CutoverCleanup` - Deleting migrated shard data from old nodes\n8. `Complete` - All shards migrated\n\n**Background Task Pattern** (`rebalancer_worker/mod.rs`):\n- Leader election via `try_acquire_leader_lease()`\n- Event-driven architecture using `TopologyChangeEvent`\n- Job persistence using `TaskStore` interface\n- Progress tracking with `ShardMigrationProgress`\n- Metrics callbacks for Prometheus integration\n\n**Async Background Workers**:\n- All workers use `tokio::spawn()` for concurrent execution\n- Leader election ensures only one pod runs critical operations\n- Channel-based communication for topology events\n- Configurable intervals and batch sizes for throttling\n\n### 6. Admin API Structure\n\n**Admin Endpoints** (`admin_endpoints.rs`):\n- `POST /_miroir/nodes` - Add node to replica group\n- `DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id}` - Remove node from cluster\n- `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain` - Drain node (prepare for removal)\n- `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/fail` - Mark node as failed\n- `POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/recover` - Mark failed node as recovered\n- `GET /_miroir/rebalance/status` - Get rebalance status\n- `GET /_miroir/topology` - Full cluster state\n- `GET /_miroir/shards` - Shard → node mapping table\n\n**Admin Session Management** (`admin_session.rs`):\n- Cookie-based session authentication\n- Rate limiting with exponential backoff\n- Constant-time admin key comparison\n\n### Key Architectural Insights:\n\n1. **Dual-Write Safety**: The system implements sophisticated dual-write logic to prevent data loss during migrations, including delta passes to catch stragglers.\n\n2. **Leader Election**: Distributed coordination using advisory locks via `TaskStore` (Redis/SQLite backends).\n\n3. **State Machine Approach**: Comprehensive state machines for both node status and migration phases with legal transitions.\n\n4. **Rendezvous Hashing**: Deterministic, consistent shard assignment that minimizes reshuffling during topology changes.\n\n5. **Event-Driven Architecture**: Background workers react to topology change events through channels.\n\n6. **Progress Persistence**: Migration progress is persisted for resumability in case of failures.\n\nThis architecture provides a solid foundation for implementing cluster membership changes and rebalancing operations with strong safety guarantees."}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d56fc62b-658d-43f7-849e-ae232bebd402","uuid":"4fbb6b33-aed8-4d13-a43f-b9d51d812618","timestamp":"2026-05-23T12:17:41.053Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Explore the miroir codebase to understand:\n1. Implements the node-addition migration flow from plan §4 with explicit state\n4\t//! transitions and a race-window-safe cutover sequence.\n5\t//!\n6\t//! ## Race window analysis (plan §15 OP#1)\n7\t//!\n8\t//! The dangerous window is between \"mark node active\" (routing changes to new-node-only)\n9\t//! and \"delete migrated shard from old node.\" A document written during dual-write that\n10\t//! succeeded on OLD but failed on NEW — and arrived after the last migration page —\n11\t//! would be deleted from OLD without ever reaching NEW.\n12\t//!\n13\t//! ## Solution: quiesce-then-verify cutover\n14\t//!\n15\t//! Instead of the naïve sequence (mark active → stop dual-write → delete old), we use:\n16\t//!\n17\t//! 1. Stop dual-write (no new writes go to either node for affected shards)\n18\t//! 2. Drain: wait for all in-flight writes to both OLD and NEW to complete\n19\t//! 3. Delta migration: re-read affected shards from OLD (catches anything written since\n20\t//!    the last migration page) and write deltas to NEW\n21\t//! 4. Mark node active (routing switches to NEW-only)\n22\t//! 5. Delete migrated shard from OLD\n23\t//!\n24\t//! Step 3 is the key: it closes the race window by ensuring NEW has a complete picture\n25\t//! before we commit the routing change. The cost is one extra pagination pass over each\n26\t//! migrated shard — bounded by the number of docs written during the migration window.\n27\t\n28\tuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\n29\tuse std::fmt;\n30\tuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n31\t\n32\tuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n33\t\n34\t/// Unique identifier for a shard migration
