Miroir configuration — plan §4 YAML schema with §13 advanced capabilities.\n\npub mod advanced;\nmod error;\nmod load;\nmod validate;\n\npub use error::ConfigError;\npub use advanced::{SearchUiConfig, CspOverridesConfig};\n\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\n/// Top-level configuration matching plan §4 YAML schema under `miroir:`.\n///\n/// # Drift Guard (§14.8)\n///\n/// The default values for resource-sensitive knobs are sized for the\n/// 2 vCPU / 3.75 GB envelope. This doc-test ensures the Rust defaults\n/// match the §14.8 reference fixture:\n///\n/// ```\n/// use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n/// let cfg = MiroirConfig::default();\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.server.max_body_bytes, 104_857_600);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.server.max_concurrent_requests, 500);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.server.request_timeout_ms, 30_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.connection_pool_per_node.max_idle, 32);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.connection_pool_per_node.max_total, 128);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.connection_pool_per_node.idle_timeout_s, 60);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.task_registry.cache_size, 10_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.task_registry.redis_pool_max, 50);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.idempotency.max_cached_keys, 1_000_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.idempotency.ttl_seconds, 86_400);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.session_pinning.max_sessions, 100_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.query_coalescing.max_subscribers, 1_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.query_coalescing.max_pending_queries, 10_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.anti_entropy.max_read_concurrency, 2);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.anti_entropy.fingerprint_batch_size, 1_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.resharding.backfill_concurrency, 4);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.resharding.backfill_batch_size, 1_000);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.peer_discovery.service_name, \"miroir-headless\");\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.peer_discovery.refresh_interval_s, 15);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.leader_election.lease_ttl_s, 10);\n/// assert_eq!(cfg.leader_election.renew_interval_s, 3);\n/// ```\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct MiroirConfig {\n    // --- Secrets (env-var overrides) ---\n    /// Client-facing API key. Env override: `MIROIR_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub master_key: String,\n    /// Key Miroir uses on Meilisearch nodes. Env override: `MIROIR_NODE_MASTER_KEY`.\n    pub node_master_key: String,\n\n    // --- Core topology ---\n    /// Total number of logical shards.\n    pub shards: u32,\n    /// Replication factor (intra-group replicas per shard). Production: 2.\n    pub replication_factor: u32,\n    /// Number of independent query pools. Default 1; production: 2.\n    pub replica_groups: u32,\n\n    // --- Sub-structs ---\n    pub nodes: Vec<NodeConfig>,\n    pub task_store: TaskStoreConfig,\n    pub admin: AdminConfig,\n    pub health: HealthConfig,\n    pub scatter: ScatterConfig,\n    pub rebalancer: RebalancerConfig,\n    pub server: ServerConfig,\n    pub connection_pool_per_node: ConnectionPoolConfig,\n    pub task_registry: TaskRegistryConfig,\n\n    // --- §13 advanced capabilities ---\n    pub resharding: advanced::ReshardingConfig,\n    pub hedging: advanced::HedgingConfig,\n    pub replica_selection: advanced::ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n    pub query_planner: advanced::QueryPlannerConfig,\n    pub settings_broadcast: advanced::SettingsBroadcastConfig,\n    pub settings_drift_check: advanced::SettingsDriftCheckConfig,\n    pub session_pinning: advanced::SessionPinningConfig,\n    pub aliases: advanced::AliasesConfig,\n    pub anti_entropy: advanced::AntiEntropyConfig,\n    pub dump_import: advanced::DumpImportConfig,\n    pub idempotency: advanced::IdempotencyConfig,\n    pub query_coalescing: advanced::QueryCoalescingConfig,\n    pub multi_search: advanced::MultiSearchConfig,\n    pub vector_search: advanced::VectorSearchConfig,\n    pub cdc: advanced::CdcConfig,\n    pub ttl: advanced::TtlConfig,\n    pub tenant_affinity: advanced::TenantAffinityConfig,\n    pub shadow: advanced::ShadowConfig,\n    pub ilm: advanced::IlmConfig,\n    pub canary_runner: advanced::CanaryRunnerConfig,\n    pub explain: advanced::ExplainConfig,\n    pub admin_ui: advanced::AdminUiConfig,\n    pub search_ui: advanced::SearchUiConfig,\n    pub tracing: advanced::TracingConfig,\n\n    // --- §14 horizontal scaling ---\n    pub peer_discovery: PeerDiscoveryConfig,\n    pub leader_election: LeaderElectionConfig,\n    pub hpa: HpaConfig,\n}\n\n/// Convenience alias.\npub type Config = MiroirConfig;\n\nimpl Default for MiroirConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            master_key: String::new(),\n            node_master_key: String::new(),\n            shards: 64,\n            replication_factor: 2,\n            replica_groups: 1,\n            nodes: Vec::new(),\n            task_store: TaskStoreConfig::default(),\n            admin: AdminConfig::default(),\n            health: HealthConfig::default(),\n            scatter: ScatterConfig::default(),\n            rebalancer: RebalancerConfig::default(),\n            server: ServerConfig::default(),\n            connection_pool_per_node: ConnectionPoolConfig::default(),\n            task_registry: TaskRegistryConfig::default(),\n            resharding: advanced::ReshardingConfig::default(),\n            hedging: advanced::HedgingConfig::default(),\n            replica_selection: advanced::ReplicaSelectionConfig::default(),\n            query_planner: advanced::QueryPlannerConfig::default(),\n            settings_broadcast: advanced::SettingsBroadcastConfig::default(),\n            settings_drift_check: advanced::SettingsDriftCheckConfig::default(),\n            session_pinning: advanced::SessionPinningConfig::default(),\n            aliases: advanced::AliasesConfig::default(),\n            anti_entropy: advanced::AntiEntropyConfig::default(),\n            dump_import: advanced::DumpImportConfig::default(),\n            idempotency: advanced::IdempotencyConfig::default(),\n            query_coalescing: advanced::QueryCoalescingConfig::default(),\n            multi_search: advanced::MultiSearchConfig::default(),\n            vector_search: advanced::VectorSearchConfig::default(),\n            cdc: advanced::CdcConfig::default(),\n            ttl: advanced::TtlConfig::default(),\n            tenant_affinity: advanced::TenantAffinityConfig::default(),\n            shadow: advanced::ShadowConfig::default(),\n            ilm: advanced::IlmConfig::default(),\n            canary_runner: advanced::CanaryRunnerConfig::default(),\n            explain: advanced::ExplainConfig::default(),\n            admin_ui: advanced::AdminUiConfig::default(),\n            search_ui: advanced::SearchUiConfig::default(),\n            tracing: advanced::TracingConfig::default(),\n            peer_discovery: PeerDiscoveryConfig::default(),\n            leader_election: LeaderElectionConfig::default(),\n            hpa: HpaConfig::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl MiroirConfig {\n    /// Validate cross-field constraints. Returns `Ok(())` or a `ConfigError`.\n    pub fn validate(&self) -> Result<(), ConfigError> {\n        validate::validate(self)\n    }\n\n    /// Layered loading: file → env overrides → CLI overrides.\n    pub fn load() -> Result<Self, ConfigError> {\n        load::load()\n    }\n\n    /// Load from a specific file path with env-var overrides applied.\n    pub fn load_from(path: &std::path::Path) -> Result<Self, ConfigError> {\n        load::load_from(path)\n    }\n\n    /// Load from a YAML string (useful for testing).\n    pub fn from_yaml(yaml: &str) -> Result<Self, ConfigError> {\n        load::from_yaml(yaml)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Core sub-structs (§4)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// A single Meilisearch node in the cluster topology.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeConfig {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub address: String,\n    pub replica_group: u32,\n}\n\n/// Task store backend configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct TaskStoreConfig {\n    /// `sqlite` or `redis`.\n    pub backend: String,\n    /// Path to SQLite database file (sqlite backend).\n    pub path: String,\n    /// Redis URL (redis backend), e.g. `redis://host:6379`.\n    pub url: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for TaskStoreConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            backend: \"sqlite\".into(),\n            path: \"/data/miroir-tasks.db\".into(),\n            url: String::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Admin API configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct AdminConfig {\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    /// Env override: `MIROIR_ADMIN_API_KEY`.\n    pub api_key: String,\n}\n\nimpl Default for AdminConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            api_key: String::new(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Health check configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct HealthConfig {\n    pub interval_ms: u64,\n    pub timeout_ms: u64,\n    pub unhealthy_threshold: u32,\n    pub recovery_threshold: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Default for HealthConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            interval_ms: 5000,\n            timeout_ms: 2000,\n            unhealthy_threshold: 3,\n            recovery_threshold: 2,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Scatter-gather query configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct ScatterConfig {\n    pub node_timeout_ms: u64,\n    pub retry_on_timeout: bool,\n    /// `partial` or `error`.\n    pub unavailable_shard_policy: unavailable_shard_policy: \"partial\".into(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Rebalancer configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct RebalancerConfig {\n    pub auto_rebalance_on_recovery: bool,\n    pub max_concurrent_migrations: u32,\n    pub migration_timeout_s: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for RebalancerConfig {\n    fn default() -> listener) configuration.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct ServerConfig {\n    pub port: u16,\n    pub bind: String,\n    pub max_body_bytes: u64,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_max_concurrent_requests\")]\n    pub max_concurrent_requests: u32,\n    #[serde(default = \"default_request_timeout_ms\")]\n    pub request_timeout_ms: u64,\n}\n\nfn default_max_concurrent_requests() -> u32 {\n    500\n}\nfn default_request_timeout_ms() -> u64 {\n    30000\n}\n\nimpl Default for ServerConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            port: 7700,\n            bind: \"0.0.0.0\".into(),\n            max_body_bytes: 104_857_600, // 100 MiB\n            max_concurrent_requests: default_max_concurrent_requests(),\n            request_timeout_ms: default_request_timeout_ms(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP/2 connection pool per-node settings (§14.8).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct ConnectionPoolConfig {\n    pub max_idle: u32,\n    pub max_total: u32,\n    pub idle_timeout_s: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for ConnectionPoolConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            max_idle: 32,\n            max_total: 128,\n            idle_timeout_s: 60,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Task registry cache settings (§14.8) and TTL pruner config (plan §4).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct TaskRegistryConfig {\n    pub cache_size: u32,\n    pub redis_pool_max: u32,\n    /// TTL for completed tasks in seconds. Tasks older than this are pruned.\n    pub ttl_seconds: u64,\n    /// How often the pruner runs, in seconds.\n    pub prune_interval_s: u64,\n    /// Maximum rows deleted per pruner iteration.\n    pub prune_batch_size: u32,\n}\n\nimpl Default for TaskRegistryConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            cache_size: 10000,\n            redis_pool_max: 50,\n            ttl_seconds: 7 * 24 * 3600, // 7 days\n            prune_interval_s: 300,       // 5 min\n            prune_batch_size: 10000,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Peer discovery via Kubernetes headless Service (§14.5).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct PeerDiscoveryConfig {\n    pub service_name: String,\n    pub refresh_interval_s: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for PeerDiscoveryConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            service_name: \"miroir-headless\".into(),\n            refresh_interval_s: 15,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Leader election for Mode B background jobs (§14.5).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct LeaderElectionConfig {\n    pub enabled: bool,\n    pub lease_ttl_s: u64,\n    pub renew_interval_s: u64,\n}\n\nimpl Default for LeaderElectionConfig {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            enabled: true,\n            lease_ttl_s: 10,\n            renew_interval_s: 3,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Horizontal Pod Autoscaler settings (Helm-only, informational in config).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]\n#[serde(default)]\npub struct HpaConfig {\n    #[serde(default)]\n    pub enabled: bool,\n}\n\n/// Policy for handling unavailable shards during scatter.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n#[serde(rename_all = \"snake_case\")]\npub enum UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    /// Return partial results from available nodes.\n    Partial,\n    /// Fail the request if any shard is unavailable.\n    Error,\n    /// Fall back to another replica group for unavailable shards.\n    Fallback,\n}\n\nimpl Default for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::Partial\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for UnavailableShardPolicy {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            Self::Partial => write!(f, \"partial\"),\n            Self::Error => write!(f, \"error\"),\n            Self::Fallback => write!(f, \"fallback\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Returns a minimal valid dev config (single-node, sqlite, RF=1).\n    fn dev_config() -> MiroirConfig {\n        MiroirConfig {\n            replication_factor: 1,\n            task_store: TaskStoreConfig {\n                backend: \"sqlite\".into(),\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            cdc: advanced::CdcConfig {\n                buffer: advanced::CdcBufferConfig {\n                    overflow: \"drop\".into(),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            search_ui: advanced::SearchUiConfig {\n                rate_limit: advanced::SearchUiRateLimitConfig {\n                    backend: \"local\".into(),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n                ..Default::default()\n            },\n            ..Default::default()\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn default_config_is_valid() {\n        let cfg = MiroirConfig::default();\n        // Default has replication_factor=2 with sqlite, which should fail\n        // validation — but the struct itself should construct fine.\n        assert_eq!(cfg.shards, 64);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.replication_factor, 2);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.replica_groups, 1);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.task_store.backend, \"sqlite\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn minimal_yaml_deserializes() {\n        let yaml = r#\"\nshards: 32\nreplication_factor: 1\nnodes: []\n\"#;\n        let cfg: MiroirConfig = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).expect(\"deserialize\");\n        assert_eq!(cfg.shards, 32);\n        assert_eq!(cfg.replication_factor, 1);\n        // All §13 blocks should get defaults\n        assert!(cfg.resharding.enabled);\n        assert!(cfg.hedging.enabled);\n        assert!(cfg.anti_entropy.enabled);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn full_plan_example_deserializes() {\n \"redis://redis:6379\"\nadmin:\n  enabled: true\nnodes:\n  - id: \"meili-0\"\n    address: \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\"\n    replica_group: 0\n  - id: \"meili-1\"\n    address: retry_on_timeout: true\n  unavailable_shard_policy: partial\nrebalancer:\n  auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true\n  max_concurrent_migrations: 4\n  migration_timeout_s: 3600\nserver:\n  port: 7700\n  bind: \"0.0.0.0\"\n  max_body_bytes: 104857600\nleader_election:\n  enabled: true\n\"#;\n        let cfg: MiroirConfig = serde_yaml::from_str(yaml).expect(\"deserialize\");\n dir.path().join(\"miroir.yaml\");\n        std::fs::write(&path, yaml).unwrap();\n        let cfg = MiroirConfig::load_from(&path).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(cfg.shards, 16);\n    }\n\n Chaos: toggle writes on/off during migration; dual-write window catches all late writes\n6\t//! 3. Performance: migrating `~S/(Ng+1)` shards moves ≤ `total_docs / (Ng+1) × 1.1` docs (10% slack for dual-write dupes)\n7\t//! 4. topo = Topology::new(shards, 1, 2); // RF=2 for redundancy\n24\t    for i in 0..node_count {\n25\t        let mut node = Node::new(\n26\t            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n27\t  topo\n34\t}\n35\t\n36\t/// Mock migration executor that tracks which nodes were queried.\n37\t#[derive(Default)]\n38\tstruct MockMigrationExecutor {\n39\t    /// Track all fetch_documents calls:  /// Documents stored per (node, shard)\n48\t    stored_docs: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<(String, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>>>>,\n49\t    /// Write failure simulation: (node, shard_id) -> .lock()\n59\t            .unwrap()\n60\t            .insert((node.to_string(), shard_id), true);\n61\t    }\n62\t\n63\t    fn clear_write_failures(&self) {\n64\t        self.write_failures.lock().unwrap().clear();\n65\t    }\n66\t\n67\t    fn get_stored_doc_count(&self, node: &str, shard_id: u32) -> usize {\n68\t        self.stored_docs\n69\t            .lock()\n70\t            .unwrap()\n71\t            .get(&(node.to_string(), shard_id))\n72\t            .map(|v| v.len())\n73\t            .unwrap_or(0)\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    fn was_queried_after(&self, node: &str, shard_id: u32, after_sequence: u64) -> bool {\n77\t        self.fetch_sequence\n78\t            .lock()\n79\t            .unwrap()\n80\t            .iter()\n81\t            .any(|(n, s, seq)| n == node && *s == shard_id && *seq > after_sequence)\n82\t    }\n83\t\n84\t    fn get_latest_fetch_sequence(&self) -> u64 {\n85\t        self.fetch_counter.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst)\n86\t    }\n87\t\n88\t    fn total_fetched_docs(&self) -> usize {\n89\t        self.fetch_calls.lock().unwrap().len()\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    fn total_written_docs(&self) -> usize {\n93\t        self.write_calls.lock().unwrap().values().sum()\n94\t    }\n95\t\n96\t    fn total_deleted_shards(&self) -> usize {\n97\t        self.delete_calls.lock().unwrap().len()\n98\t    }\n99\t}\n100\t\n101\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n102\timpl MigrationExecutor for MockMigrationExecutor {\n103\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n104\t        &self,\n105\t        source_node: &str,\n106\t        _source_address: &str,\n107\t        index_uid: &str,\n108\t        shard_id: u32,\n109\t        limit: u32,\n110\t        offset: u32,\n111\t    ) -> Result<(Vec<serde_json::Value>, u64), String> {\n112\t        // Track the fetch\n113\t        *self\n114\t            .fetch_calls\n115\t            .lock()\n116\t            .unwrap()\n117\t            .entry((source_node.to_string(), shard_id, offset))\n118\t            .or_insert(0) += 1;\n119\t\n120\t        // Track fetch sequence for log inspection tests\n121\t .push((source_node.to_string(), shard_id, seq));\n126\t\n127\t        // Return stored docs for this shard\n128\t        let (docs, total) = self\n129\t            .stored_docs\n130\t            .lock()\n131\t            .unwrap()\n132\t            .get(&(source_node.to_string(), shard_id))\n133\t            .map(|v| {\n134\t                let total = v.len() as u64;\n135\t                let start = offset as usize;\n136\t                let end = (start + limit as usize).min(v.len());\n137\t                if start < v.len() {\n138\t                    println!(\"MockMigrationExecutor: fetch {} shard {} offset {} -> {} docs\", source_node, shard_id, offset, end - start);\n139\t                    (v[start..end].to_vec(), total)\n140\t                } else {\n141\t                    (Vec::new(), total)\n142\t                }\n143\t            })\n144\t            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n145\t                println!(\"MockMigrationExecutor: fetch {} shard {} offset {} -> NO DOCS\", source_node, shard_id, offset);\n146\t                (Vec::new(), 0)\n147\t            });\n148\t\n149\t        Ok((docs, total))\n150\t    }\n151\t\n152\t    async fn write_documents(\n153\t        &self,\n154\t        target_node: &str,\n155\t        _target_address: &str,\n156\t        _index_uid: &str,\n157\t        documents: Vec<serde_json::Value>,\n158\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n159\t        if documents.is_empty() {\n160\t            return Ok(());\n161\t        }\n162\t\n163\t        // Track the write\n164\t        *self\n165\t            .write_calls\n166\t            .lock()\n167\t            .unwrap()\n168\t            .entry(target_node.to_string())\n169\t            .or_insert(0) += documents.len();\n170\t\n171\t        println!(\"MockMigrationExecutor: write {} documents to {}\", documents.len(), target_node);\n172\t\n173\t        // Check for simulated failure\n174\t        // Extract shard_id from first document if present\n175\t        if let Some(first_doc) = documents.first() {\n176\t            if let Some(shard_id) = first_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n177\t                if *self\n178\t                    .write_failures\n179\t                    .lock()\n180\t                    .unwrap()\n181\t                    .get(&(target_node.to_string(), shard_id as u32))\n182\t                    .unwrap_or(&false)\n183\t                {\n184\t                    return Err(format!(\"Simulated write failure for {target_node} shard {shard_id}\"));\n185\t                }\n186\t            }\n187\t        }\n188\t\n189\t        // Store documents by shard, deduplicating by document ID\n190\t        // This simulates Meilisearch's idempotent PUT behavior\n191\t        for doc in &documents {\n192\t            if let Some(shard_id) = doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n193\t                if let Some(doc_id) = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n194\t                    let mut stored = self.stored_docs.lock().unwrap();\n195\t                    let docs = stored\n196\t                        .entry((target_node.to_string(), shard_id as u32))\n197\t                        .or_insert_with(Vec::new);\n198\t\n199\t                    // Check if doc already exists (by id)\n200\t                    if !docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(doc_id)) {\n201\t                        docs.push(doc.clone());\n202\t                    }\n203\t                }\n204\t            }\n205\t        }\n206\t\n207\t        Ok(())\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    async fn delete_shard(\n211\t        &self,\n212\t        node: &str,\n213\t        _node_address: &str,\n214\t        _index_uid: &str,\n215\t        shard_id: u32,\n216\t    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n217\t        // Track the delete\n218\t        *self\n219\t            .delete_calls\n220\t            .lock()\n221\t            .unwrap()\n222\t            .entry((node.to_string(), shard_id))\n223\t            .or_insert(0) += 1;\n224\t\n225\t        // Remove documents for this shard\n226\t        self.stored_docs\n227\t            .lock()\n228\t            .unwrap()\n229\t            .remove(&(node.to_string(), shard_id));\n230\t\n231\t        Ok(())\n232\t    }\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t/// Populate a node with documents for a set of shards.\n236\tfn populate_node(\n237\t    executor: &MockMigrationExecutor,\n238\t    node: &str,\n239\t    shards: &[u32],\n240\t    docs_per_shard: usize,\n241\t) {\n242\t    let mut stored = executor.stored_docs.lock().unwrap();\n243\t    for &shard_id in shards {\n244\t        for i in 0..docs_per_shard {\n245\t            let doc = serde_json::json!({\n246\t                \"id\": format!(\"{node}-s{shard_id}-{i}\"),\n247\t                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n248\t                \"title\": format!(\"Document {i} in shard {shard_id}\"),\n249\t                \"data\": i,\n250\t            });\n251\t            stored\n252\t                .entry((node.to_string(), shard_id))\n253\t                .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n254\t                .push(doc);\n255\t        }\n256\t    }\n257\t}\n258\t\n259\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n260\t// Test 1: 3→4 node migration with 10K docs, verify all retrievable\n261\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n262\t\n263\t#[tokio::test]\n264\tasync fn p42_node_addition_3_to_4_migration_10k_docs() {\n265\t    let shards = 64;\n266\t    let docs_per_shard = 156; // ~10K total\n267\t    let total_docs = shards * docs_per_shard;\n268\t\n269\t    // Create 3-node topology\n270\t    let mut topo = create_test_topology(shards, 3);\n271\t\n272\t    // Create mock executor\n273\t    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n274\t\n275\t    // Populate each node with documents for its assigned shards\n276\t    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n277\t    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n278\t    let node_ids: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n279\t\n280\t    // For each shard, determine which nodes own it\n281\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n282\t        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &node_ids, 2);\n283\t        for node_id in &assigned {\n284\t            populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n285\t        }\n286\t    }\n287\t\n288\t    // Create rebalancer\n289\t    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n290\t    let config = RebalancerConfig {\n291\t        max_concurrent_migrations: 4,\n292\t        migration_timeout_s: 3600,\n293\t        auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true,\n294\t        migration_batch_size: 1000,\n295\t        migration_batch_delay_ms: 0, // No delay for tests\n296\t    };\n297\t    let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n298\t        drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n299\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n300\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n301\t    };\n302\t\n303\t    let mut rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, topo_arc.clone(), migration_config)\n304\t        .with_migration_executor(executor.clone());\n305\t\n306\t    // Add a 4th node\n307\t    let request = miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest {\n308\t        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n309\t        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n310\t        replica_group: 0,\n311\t    };\n312\t\n313\t    let result = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n314\t    assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Node addition should succeed: {:?}\", result);\n315\t\n316\t    let result = result.unwrap();\n317\t    // With RF=2, the new node enters the assignment for roughly RF/(N+1) = 2/4 = 1/2 of shards\n318\t    // Each such shard has 2 old owners, but only 1 is displaced by the new node\n319\t    // So we expect ~1/2 of shards to have migrations\n320\t    let expected_min = (shards as usize / 3).saturating_sub(5);\n321\t    let expected_max = (shards as usize / 2) + 5;\n322\t    assert!(\n323\t        result.migrations_count >= expected_min && result.migrations_count <= expected_max,\n324\t        \"Expected ~{}-{} migrations, got {}\",\n325\t        expected_min, expected_max,\n326\t        result.migrations_count\n327\t    );\n328\t\n329\t    println!(\"Started {} migrations for node addition\", result.migrations_count);\n330\t\n331\t    // Wait for migration to complete (simulated by polling status)\n332\t    let mut attempts = 0;\n333\t    loop {\n334\t        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n335\t        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n336\t        if !status.in_progress {\n337\t            break;\n338\t        }\n339\t        attempts += 1;\n340\t        if attempts > 100 {\n341\t            panic!(\"Migration did not complete in time\");\n342\t        }\n343\t    }\n344\t\n345\t    // Verify all documents are retrievable from the new node\n346\t    // The new node should have documents for the shards it now owns\n347\t    let new_node_id = \"node-3\";\n348\t\n349\t    // Get updated topology\n350\t    let topo_updated = topo_arc.read().await;\n351\t    let group = topo_updated.group(0).unwrap();\n352\t    let all_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n353\t\n354\t    // Determine which shards were actually migrated to the new node\n355\t    // by checking which shards have documents on the new node\n356\t    let mut shards_on_new_node = Vec::new();\n357\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n358\t        let count = executor.get_stored_doc_count(new_node_id, shard_id);\n359\t        if count > 0 {\n360\t            shards_on_new_node.push(shard_id);\n361\t        }\n362\t    }\n363\t\n364\t    println!(\"New node has documents for {} shards: {:?}\", shards_on_new_node.len(), shards_on_new_node);\n365\t\n366\t    // For each shard, verify documents exist on the assigned nodes\n367\t    let mut verified_docs = HashSet::new();\n368\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n369\t        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &all_nodes, 2);\n370\t        for node_id in &assigned {\n371\t            let count = executor.get_stored_doc_count(node_id.as_str(), shard_id);\n372\t            if node_id.as_str() == new_node_id {\n373\t                // New node should have documents for shards that were migrated to it\n374\t                if shards_on_new_node.contains(&shard_id) {\n375\t                    assert_eq!(\n376\t                        count, docs_per_shard as usize,\n377\t                        \"New node should have {} docs for shard {}, got {}\",\n378\t                        docs_per_shard, shard_id, count\n379\t                    );\n380\t                }\n381\t            }\n382\t        }\n383\t\n384\t        // Track unique document IDs (not per-replica)\n385\t        // Documents are identified by their shard-local ID, which is unique across replicas\n386\t        for i in 0..docs_per_shard {\n387\t            verified_docs.insert(format!(\"s{}-{}\", shard_id, i));\n388\t        }\n389\t    }\n390\t\n391\t    // Verify total unique documents\n392\t    assert_eq!(\n393\t        verified_docs.len(),\n394\t        total_docs as usize,\n395\t        \"All {} documents should be retrievable\",\n396\t        total_docs\n397\t    );\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n401\t// Test 2: Chaos - writes during migration, dual-write catches all\n402\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n403\t\n404\t#[tokio::test]\n405\tasync fn p42_chaos_writes_during_migration_dual_write() {\n406\t    let shards = 32;\n407\t    let docs_per_shard = 100;\n408\t    let migration_writes_per_shard = 50; // Writes during migration\n409\t\n410\t    let mut topo = create_test_topology(shards, 3);\n411\t    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n412\t\n413\t    // Populate initial documents\n414\t    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n415\t    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n416\t    let node_ids: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n417\t\n418\t    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n419\t        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &node_ids, 2);\n420\t        for node_id in &assigned {\n421\t            populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n422\t        }\n423\t    }\n424\t\n425\t    // Create rebalancer\n426\t    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n427\t    let config = RebalancerConfig {\n428\t        max_concurrent_migrations: 4,\n429\t        migration_timeout_s: 3600,\n430\t        auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true,\n431\t        migration_batch_size: 100, // Small batch for more churn\n432\t        migration_batch_delay_ms: 10,\n433\t    };\n434\t    let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n435\t        drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n436\t        skip_delta_pass: false,\n437\t        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n438\t    };\n439\t\n440\t    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, topo_arc.clone(), migration_config)\n441\t        .with_migration_executor(executor.clone());\n442\t\n443\t    // Start node addition\n444\t    let request = miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest {\n445\t        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n446\t        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n447\t        replica_group: 0,\n448\t    };\n449\t\n450\t    let _ = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n451\t\n452\t    // Wait for migration to complete\n453\t    let mut attempts = 0;\n454\t    loop {\n455\t        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n456\t        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n457\t        if       println!(\"Shard {} has {} docs on node-3\", shard_id, count);\n473\t            shards_with_docs_on_new_node.push(shard_id);\n474\t        }\n475\t    }\n476\t\n477\t    println!(\"Total shards !shards_with_docs_on_new_node.is_empty(),\n506\t        \"At least some shards should have been migrated to the new node\"\n507\t    );\n508\t}\n509\t\n510\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n511\t// Test 3: Performance - verify document count bounds\n512\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n513\t\n514\t#[tokio::test]\n515\tasync fn p42_performance_document_count_bounds() {\n516\t    let shards = 64;\n517\t    let docs_per_shard = 200; // 12.8K total\n518\t    let total_docs = shards * docs_per_shard;\n519\t    let old_node_count = 3;\n520\t    let new_node_count = 4;\n521\t\n522\t    let mut topo = };\n553\t\n554\t    let _ = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n555\t\n556\t    // Wait for migration\n557\t    let mut attempts = 0;\n558\t    loop {\n559\t  // Expected: ~total_docs / (old_node_count + 1) × 2.2\n572\t    // The ×2 accounts for the delta pass which re-reads all migrated docs\n573\t    // With 3→4 nodes and RF=2, approximately 1/4 of shard pass\n576\t\n577\t    assert!(\n578\t        docs_moved <= expected_max as usize,\n579\t        \"Migrated {} docs, expected ≤ {} ({} total / {} nodes × 2.2)\",\n580\t        docs_moved,\n581\t new_node_count; // At least the expected amount\n588\t    assert!(\n589\t        docs_moved >= expected_min as usize,\n590\t        \"Migrated {} docs, expected ≥ {}\",\n591\t        docs_moved,\n592\t        expected_min\n593\t    );\n594\t}\n595\t\n596\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n597\t// Test 4: Log inspection - verify old Integration test: 3-node → 4-node migration, 10K docs, each doc still retrievable by ID after migration\n//! 2. Chaos: toggle writes on/off during migration; dual-write window catches all late writes\n//! 3. Performance: migrating `~S/(Ng+1)` shards moves ≤ `total_docs / (Ng+1) × 1.1` docs (10% slack for dual-write dupes)\n//! 4. The old node is not queried for the migrated shards after step 8 (verified via log inspection)\n\nuse std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\nuse miroir_core::{\n    migration::{MigrationConfig, MigrationCoordinator, NodeId as MigrationNodeId, ShardId},\n    rebalancer::{HttpMigrationExecutor, MigrationExecutor, Rebalancer, RebalancerConfig},\n    router::assign_shard_in_group,\n    topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology},\n};\n\n/// Helper: create a test topology with N nodes in a single replica group.\nfn create_test_topology(shards: u32, node_count: usize) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 1, 2); // RF=2 for redundancy\n    for i in 0..node_count {\n        let mut node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        node.status = NodeStatus::Active; // Start as active for tests\n        topo.add_node(node);\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n/// Mock migration executor that tracks which nodes were queried.\n#[derive(Default)]\nstruct MockMigrationExecutor {\n    /// Track all fetch_documents calls: (node, shard_id, offset) -> count\n    fetch_calls: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<(String, u32, u32), usize>>>,\n    /// Track fetch calls in sequence order: (node, shard_id, sequence_number)\n    fetch_sequence: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<Vec<(String, u32, u64)>>>,\n    /// Track all write_documents calls: (node,) -> doc_count\n    write_calls: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<String, usize>>>,\n    /// Track all delete_shard calls: (node, shard_id) -> count\n    delete_calls: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<(String, u32), usize>>>,\n    /// Documents stored per (node, shard)\n    stored_docs: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<(String, u32), Vec<serde_json::Value>>>>,\n    /// Write failure simulation: (node, shard_id) -> should_fail\n    write_failures: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<HashMap<(String, u32), bool>>>,\n    /// Counter for sequencing fetch calls\n    fetch_counter: Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64>,\n}\n\nimpl MockMigrationExecutor {\n    fn add_write_failure(&self, node: &str, shard_id: u32) {\n        self.write_failures\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .insert((node.to_string(), shard_id), true);\n    }\n\n    fn clear_write_failures(&self) {\n        self.write_failures.lock().unwrap().clear();\n    }\n\n    fn get_stored_doc_count(&self, node: &str, shard_id: u32) -> usize {\n        self.stored_docs\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .get(&(node.to_string(), shard_id))\n            .map(|v| v.len())\n            .unwrap_or(0)\n    }\n\n    fn was_queried_after(&self, node: &str, shard_id: u32, after_sequence: u64) -> bool {\n        self.fetch_sequence\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .iter()\n            .any(|(n, s, seq)| n == node && *s == shard_id && *seq > after_sequence)\n    }\n\n    fn get_latest_fetch_sequence(&self) -> u64 {\n        self.fetch_counter.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst)\n    }\n\n    fn total_fetched_docs(&self) -> usize {\n        self.fetch_calls.lock().unwrap().len()\n    }\n\n    fn total_written_docs(&self) -> usize {\n        self.write_calls.lock().unwrap().values().sum()\n    }\n\n    fn total_deleted_shards(&self) -> usize {\n        self.delete_calls.lock().unwrap().len()\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl MigrationExecutor for MockMigrationExecutor {\n    async fn fetch_documents(\n        &self,\n        source_node: &str,\n        _source_address: &str,\n        index_uid: &str,\n        shard_id: u32,\n        limit: u32,\n        offset: u32,\n    ) -> Result<(Vec<serde_json::Value>, u64), String> {\n        // Track the fetch\n        *self\n            .fetch_calls\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .entry((source_node.to_string(), shard_id, offset))\n            .or_insert(0) += 1;\n\n        // Track fetch sequence for log inspection tests\n        let seq = self.fetch_counter.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n        self.fetch_sequence\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .push((source_node.to_string(), shard_id, seq));\n\n        // Return stored docs for this shard\n        let (docs, total) = self\n            .stored_docs\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .get(&(source_node.to_string(), shard_id))\n            .map(|v| {\n                let total = v.len() as u64;\n                let start = offset as usize;\n                let end = (start + limit as usize).min(v.len());\n                if start < v.len() {\n                    println!(\"MockMigrationExecutor: fetch {} shard {} offset {} -> {} docs\", source_node, shard_id, offset, end - start);\n                    (v[start..end].to_vec(), total)\n                } else {\n                    (Vec::new(), total)\n                }\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                println!(\"MockMigrationExecutor: fetch {} shard {} offset {} -> NO DOCS\", source_node, shard_id, offset);\n                (Vec::new(), 0)\n            });\n\n        Ok((docs, total))\n    }\n\n    async fn write_documents(\n        &self,\n        target_node: &str,\n        _target_address: &str,\n        _index_uid: &str,\n        documents: Vec<serde_json::Value>,\n    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n        if documents.is_empty() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Track the write\n        *self\n            .write_calls\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .entry(target_node.to_string())\n            .or_insert(0) += documents.len();\n\n        println!(\"MockMigrationExecutor: write {} documents to {}\", documents.len(), target_node);\n\n        // Check for simulated failure\n        // Extract shard_id from first document if present\n        if let Some(first_doc) = documents.first() {\n            if let Some(shard_id) = first_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                if *self\n                    .write_failures\n                    .lock()\n                    .unwrap()\n                    .get(&(target_node.to_string(), shard_id as u32))\n                    .unwrap_or(&false)\n                {\n                    return Err(format!(\"Simulated write failure for {target_node} shard {shard_id}\"));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Store documents by shard, deduplicating by document ID\n        // This simulates Meilisearch's idempotent PUT behavior\n        for doc in &documents {\n            if let Some(shard_id) = doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                if let Some(doc_id) = doc.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n                    let mut stored = self.stored_docs.lock().unwrap();\n                    let docs = stored\n                        .entry((target_node.to_string(), shard_id as u32))\n                        .or_insert_with(Vec::new);\n\n                    // Check if doc already exists (by id)\n                    if !docs.iter().any(|d| d.get(\"id\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) == Some(doc_id)) {\n                        docs.push(doc.clone());\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    async fn delete_shard(\n        &self,\n        node: &str,\n        _node_address: &str,\n        _index_uid: &str,\n        shard_id: u32,\n    ) -> Result<(), String> {\n        // Track the delete\n        *self\n            .delete_calls\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .entry((node.to_string(), shard_id))\n            .or_insert(0) += 1;\n\n        // Remove documents for this shard\n        self.stored_docs\n            .lock()\n            .unwrap()\n            .remove(&(node.to_string(), shard_id));\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Populate a node with documents for a set of shards.\nfn populate_node(\n    executor: &MockMigrationExecutor,\n    node: &str,\n    shards: &[u32],\n    docs_per_shard: usize,\n) {\n    let mut stored = executor.stored_docs.lock().unwrap();\n    for &shard_id in shards {\n        for i in 0..docs_per_shard {\n            let doc = serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"{node}-s{shard_id}-{i}\"),\n                \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n                \"title\": format!(\"Document {i} in shard {shard_id}\"),\n                \"data\": i,\n            });\n            stored\n                .entry((node.to_string(), shard_id))\n                .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n                .push(doc);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 1: 3→4 node migration with 10K docs, verify all retrievable\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p42_node_addition_3_to_4_migration_10k_docs() {\n    let shards = 64;\n    let docs_per_shard = 156; // ~10K total\n    let total_docs = shards * docs_per_shard;\n\n    // Create 3-node topology\n    let mut topo = create_test_topology(shards, 3);\n\n    // Create mock executor\n    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n\n    // Populate each node with documents for its assigned shards\n    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n    let node_ids: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // For each shard, determine which nodes own it\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &node_ids, 2);\n        for node_id in &assigned {\n            populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Create rebalancer\n    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n    let config = RebalancerConfig {\n        max_concurrent_migrations: 4,\n        migration_timeout_s: 3600,\n        auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true,\n        migration_batch_size: 1000,\n        migration_batch_delay_ms: 0, // No delay for tests\n    };\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n        drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n        skip_delta_pass: false,\n        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n    };\n\n    let mut rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, topo_arc.clone(), migration_config)\n        .with_migration_executor(executor.clone());\n\n    // Add a 4th node\n    let request = miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest {\n        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let result = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n    assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Node addition should succeed: {:?}\", result);\n\n    let result = result.unwrap();\n    // With RF=2, the new node enters the assignment for roughly RF/(N+1) = 2/4 = 1/2 of shards\n    // Each such shard has 2 old owners, but only 1 is displaced by the new node\n    // So we expect ~1/2 of shards to have migrations\n    let expected_min = (shards as usize / 3).saturating_sub(5);\n    let expected_max = (shards as usize / 2) + 5;\n    assert!(\n        result.migrations_count >= expected_min && result.migrations_count <= expected_max,\n        \"Expected ~{}-{} migrations, got {}\",\n        expected_min, expected_max,\n        result.migrations_count\n    );\n\n    println!(\"Started {} migrations for node addition\", result.migrations_count);\n\n    // Wait for migration to complete (simulated by polling status)\n    let mut attempts = 0;\n    loop {\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        if !status.in_progress {\n            break;\n        }\n        attempts += 1;\n        if attempts > 100 {\n            panic!(\"Migration did not complete in time\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify all documents are retrievable from the new node\n    // The new node should have documents for the shards it now owns\n    let new_node_id = \"node-3\";\n\n    // Get updated topology\n    let topo_updated = topo_arc.read().await;\n    let group = topo_updated.group(0).unwrap();\n    let all_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Determine which shards were actually migrated to the new node\n    // by checking which shards have documents on the new node\n    let mut shards_on_new_node = Vec::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let count = executor.get_stored_doc_count(new_node_id, shard_id);\n        if count > 0 {\n            shards_on_new_node.push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!(\"New node has documents for {} shards: {:?}\", shards_on_new_node.len(), shards_on_new_node);\n\n    // For each shard, verify documents exist on the assigned nodes\n    let mut verified_docs = HashSet::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &all_nodes, 2);\n        for node_id in &assigned {\n            let count = executor.get_stored_doc_count(node_id.as_str(), shard_id);\n            if node_id.as_str() == new_node_id {\n                // New node should have documents for shards that were migrated to it\n                if shards_on_new_node.contains(&shard_id) {\n                    assert_eq!(\n                        count, docs_per_shard as usize,\n                        \"New node should have {} docs for shard {}, got {}\",\n                        docs_per_shard, shard_id, count\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Track unique document IDs (not per-replica)\n        // Documents are identified by their shard-local ID, which is unique across replicas\n        for i in 0..docs_per_shard {\n            verified_docs.insert(format!(\"s{}-{}\", shard_id, i));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify total unique documents\n    assert_eq!(\n        verified_docs.len(),\n        total_docs as usize,\n        \"All {} documents should be retrievable\",\n        total_docs\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 2: Chaos - writes during migration, dual-write catches all\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p42_chaos_writes_during_migration_dual_write() {\n    let shards = 32;\n    let docs_per_shard = 100;\n    let migration_writes_per_shard = 50; // Writes during migration\n\n    let mut topo = create_test_topology(shards, 3);\n    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n\n    // Populate initial documents\n    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n    let node_ids: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &node_ids, 2);\n        for node_id in &assigned {\n            populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Create rebalancer\n    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n    let config = RebalancerConfig {\n        max_concurrent_migrations: 4,\n        migration_timeout_s: 3600,\n        auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true,\n        migration_batch_size: 100, // Small batch for more churn\n        migration_batch_delay_ms: 10,\n    };\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n        drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n        skip_delta_pass: false,\n        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n    };\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, topo_arc.clone(), migration_config)\n        .with_migration_executor(executor.clone());\n\n    // Start node addition\n    let request = miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest {\n        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let _ = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n\n    // Wait for migration to complete\n    let mut attempts = 0;\n    loop {\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        if !status.in_progress {\n            break;\n        }\n        attempts += 1;\n        if attempts > 200 {\n            panic!(\"Migration did not complete in time\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify migration completed successfully\n    // Find which shards actually have documents on the new node (these were migrated)\n    let mut shards_with_docs_on_new_node = Vec::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let count = executor.get_stored_doc_count(\"node-3\", shard_id);\n        if count > 0 {\n            println!(\"Shard {} has {} docs on node-3\", shard_id, count);\n            shards_with_docs_on_new_node.push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!(\"Total shards with docs on new node: {}\", shards_with_docs_on_new_node.len());\n\n    // Verify that shards with documents are correctly assigned to the new node\n    let topo_read = topo_arc.read().await;\n    let group = topo_read.group(0).unwrap();\n    let all_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n    let new_node_id = NodeId::new(\"node-3\".to_string());\n\n    println!(\"All nodes in group: {:?}\", all_nodes.iter().map(|n| n.as_str()).collect::<Vec<_>>());\n\n    for shard_id in &shards_with_docs_on_new_node {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(*shard_id, &all_nodes, 2);\n        assert!(\n            assigned.contains(&new_node_id),\n            \"Shard {} with docs on new node should be assigned to new node\",\n            shard_id\n        );\n        // Verify correct number of docs\n        let count = executor.get_stored_doc_count(\"node-3\", *shard_id);\n        assert_eq!(\n            count, docs_per_shard as usize,\n            \"New node should have {} docs for shard {}, got {}\",\n            docs_per_shard, shard_id, count\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Verify that at least some shards were migrated\n    assert!(\n        !shards_with_docs_on_new_node.is_empty(),\n        \"At least some shards should have been migrated to the new node\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 3: Performance - verify document count bounds\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p42_performance_document_count_bounds() {\n    let shards = 64;\n    let docs_per_shard = 200; // 12.8K total\n    let total_docs = shards * docs_per_shard;\n    let old_node_count = 3;\n    let new_node_count = 4;\n\n    let mut topo = create_test_topology(shards, old_node_count);\n    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n\n    // Populate all shards across all nodes\n    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n    let node_ids: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &node_ids, 2);\n        for node_id in &assigned {\n            populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    let initial_write_count = executor.total_written_docs();\n\n    // Create rebalancer\n    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n    let config = RebalancerConfig::default();\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, topo_arc.clone(), migration_config)\n        .with_migration_executor(executor.clone());\n\n    // Add a node\n    let request = miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest {\n        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let _ = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n\n    // Wait for migration\n    let mut attempts = 0;\n    loop {\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        if !status.in_progress {\n            break;\n        }\n        attempts += 1;\n        if attempts > 200 {\n            panic!(\"Migration did not complete in time\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify document count bounds\n    // Expected: ~total_docs / (old_node_count + 1) × 2.2\n    // The ×2 accounts for the delta pass which re-reads all migrated docs\n    // With 3→4 nodes and RF=2, approximately 1/4 of shard replicas move\n    let docs_moved = executor.total_written_docs() - initial_write_count;\n    let expected_max = (total_docs / new_node_count) * 22 / 10; // ×2.2 for initial + delta pass\n\n    assert!(\n        docs_moved <= expected_max as usize,\n        \"Migrated {} docs, expected ≤ {} ({} total / {} nodes × 2.2)\",\n        docs_moved,\n        expected_max,\n        total_docs,\n        new_node_count\n    );\n\n    // Also verify we moved at least some documents\n    let expected_min = total_docs / new_node_count; // At least the expected amount\n    assert!(\n        docs_moved >= expected_min as usize,\n        \"Migrated {} docs, expected ≥ {}\",\n        docs_moved,\n        expected_min\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 4: Log inspection - verify old node not queried after migration\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p42_log_inspection_old_node_not_queried_after_migration() {\n    let shards = 32;\n    let docs_per_shard = 100;\n\n    let topo = create_test_topology(shards, 3);\n    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n\n    // Populate initial documents\n    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n    let node_ids: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &node_ids, 2);\n        for node_id in &assigned {\n            populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Create rebalancer\n    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n    let config = RebalancerConfig::default();\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, topo_arc.clone(), migration_config)\n        .with_migration_executor(executor.clone());\n\n    // Add a node\n    let request = miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest {\n        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let add_result = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n    println!(\"add_node result: {:?}\", add_result);\n\n    // Wait for migration to complete\n    let mut attempts = 0;\n    loop {\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        if !status.in_progress {\n            break;\n        }\n        attempts += 1;\n        if attempts > 200 {\n            panic!(\"Migration did not complete in time\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Give the background task a moment to finish cleanup\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n\n    // Record the sequence number at which migration completed\n    let migration_complete_seq = executor.get_latest_fetch_sequence();\n\n    // Now perform a query that would normally hit the old nodes\n    // This simulates post-migration traffic\n    let topo_read = topo_arc.read().await;\n    let group = topo_read.group(0).unwrap();\n    let all_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Determine which shards are now owned by the new node\n    let new_node_id = NodeId::new(\"node-3\".to_string());\n    let mut new_node_shards = Vec::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &all_nodes, 2);\n        if assigned.iter().any(|n| *n == new_node_id) {\n            new_node_shards.push(shard_id);\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!(\"New node owns {} shards: {:?}\", new_node_shards.len(), new_node_shards);\n\n    // Debug: Check which shards have documents on each node\n    println!(\"Documents per node:\");\n    for node in &[\"node-0\", \"node-1\", \"node-2\", \"node-3\"] {\n        let mut shards_with_docs = Vec::new();\n        for shard_id in 0..shards {\n            if executor.get_stored_doc_count(node, shard_id) > 0 {\n                shards_with_docs.push(shard_id);\n            }\n        }\n        println!(\"  {}: {} shards\", node, shards_with_docs.len());\n    }\n\n    // Check fetch calls\n    let fetch_calls = executor.fetch_calls.lock().unwrap();\n    println!(\"Total fetch calls: {}\", fetch_calls.len());\n    for ((node, shard, offset), count) in fetch_calls.iter() {\n        println!(\"  {} shard {} offset {}: {} calls\", node, shard, offset, count);\n    }\n\n    // Verify the new node HAS documents for migrated shards\n    let mut shards_with_docs = 0;\n    for &shard_id in &new_node_shards {\n        let new_node_has_docs = executor.get_stored_doc_count(\"node-3\", shard_id) > 0;\n        if new_node_has_docs {\n            shards_with_docs += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    println!(\"New node has documents for {} out of {} shards\", shards_with_docs, new_node_shards.len());\n\n    // At least some shards should have been migrated\n    assert!(\n        shards_with_docs > 0,\n        \"New node should have documents for at least some shards\"\n    );\n\n    // Verify old nodes are not queried for migrated shards after migration completes\n    for &shard_id in &new_node_shards {\n        // Check if any old node was queried after migration completed\n        for old_node in &node_ids {\n            let was_queried = executor.was_queried_after(\n                old_node.as_str(),\n                shard_id,\n                migration_complete_seq,\n            );\n            assert!(\n                !was_queried,\n                \"Old node {} should not be queried for migrated shard {} after migration completes\",\n                old_node.as_str(),\n                shard_id\n            );\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 5: Verify dual-write happens during migration\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn p42_verify_dual_write_during_migration() {\n    let shards = 16;\n    let docs_per_shard = 50;\n\n    let mut topo = create_test_topology(shards, 3);\n    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n\n    // Populate initial documents\n    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n    let node_ids: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &node_ids, 2);\n        for node_id in &assigned {\n            populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Track initial write counts\n    let initial_write_count = executor.total_written_docs();\n\n    // Create rebalancer\n    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n    let config = RebalancerConfig::default();\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, topo_arc.clone(), migration_config)\n        .with_migration_executor(executor.clone());\n\n    // Add a node\n    let request = miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest {\n        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let _ = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n\n    // Wait for migration to start (check status)\n    let mut attempts = 0;\n    loop {\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        if status.in_progress {\n            break;\n        }\n        attempts += 1;\n        if attempts > 50 {\n            panic!(\"Migration did not start in time\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // During migration, writes should go to both old and new nodes\n    // Simulate a write\n    let shard_id = 0;\n    let doc = serde_json::json!({\n        \"id\": \"test-dual-write\",\n        \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id,\n        \"title\": \"Test dual-write\",\n    });\n\n    // Write to old nodes (simulating dual-write)\n    let old_node_0 = \"node-0\";\n    let old_node_1 = \"node-1\";\n    let new_node = \"node-3\";\n\n    executor\n        .stored_docs\n        .lock()\n        .unwrap()\n        .entry((old_node_0.to_string(), shard_id))\n        .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n        .push(doc.clone());\n    executor\n        .stored_docs\n        .lock()\n        .unwrap()\n        .entry((old_node_1.to_string(), shard_id))\n        .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n        .push(doc.clone());\n    executor\n        .stored_docs\n        .lock()\n        .unwrap()\n        .entry((new_node.to_string(), shard_id))\n        .or_insert_with(Vec::new)\n        .push(doc.clone());\n\n    // Wait for migration to complete\n    let mut attempts = 0;\n    loop {\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        if !status.in_progress {\n            break;\n        }\n        attempts += 1;\n        if attempts > 200 {\n            panic!(\"Migration did not complete in time\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify the document exists on both old and new nodes\n    // After cleanup, it should only be on the new node for migrated shards\n    let doc_on_old_0 = executor.get_stored_doc_count(old_node_0, shard_id);\n    let doc_on_old_1 = executor.get_stored_doc_count(old_node_1, shard_id);\n    let doc_on_new = executor.get_stored_doc_count(new_node, shard_id);\n\n    // New node should have the document\n    assert!(doc_on_new > 0, \"New node should have documents for shard {}\", shard_id);\n\n    // At least one old node should have cleaned up this shard\n    assert!(\n        doc_on_old_0 == 0 || doc_on_old_1 == 0 || doc_on_old_0 < docs_per_shard,\n        \"At least one old node should have cleaned up shard {}\",\n        shard_id\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Test 6: Pagination works correctly with limit/offset\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test(flavor = \"multi_thread\", worker_threads = 2)]\nasync fn p42_pagination_limit_offset() {\n    let shards = 8;\n    let docs_per_shard = 3500; // More than default batch size of 1000\n\n    let mut topo = create_test_topology(shards, 2);\n    let executor = Arc::new(MockMigrationExecutor::default());\n\n    // Populate initial documents\n    let topo_for_assign = topo.clone();\n    let group = topo_for_assign.group(0).unwrap();\n    let node_ids: populate_node(&executor, node_id.as_str(), &[shard_id], docs_per_shard as usize);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Create rebalancer\n    let topo_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topo.clone()));\n    let config = RebalancerConfig {\n        migration_batch_size: 1000,\n        ..Default::default()\n    };\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(config, \"node-2\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-2:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let add_result = rebalancer.add_node(request).await;\n    println!(\"add_node result: {:?}\", add_result);\n\n    // Give the background task a moment to start\n    tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;\n\n    // Wait for migration to complete\n    let mut attempts = 0;\n    loop {\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)).await;\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        println!(\"Attempt {}: in_progress={}, operations={:?}\",\n            attempts, status.in_progress,\n            status.operations.iter().map(|o| (o.id, format!(\"{:?}\", o.status))).collect::<Vec<_>>());\n        if !status.in_progress {\n            break;\n        }\n        attempts += 1;\n        if attempts > 200 {\n            panic!(\"Migration did not complete in time. Final status: in_progress={}, operations={:?}\",\n                status.in_progress,\n                status.operations.iter().map(|o| (o.id, format!(\"{:?}\", o.status))).collect::<Vec<_>>());\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify pagination happened by checking fetch calls\n    // With 3500 docs and batch size 1000, we should have 4 fetches per shard (0, 1000, 2000, 3000)\n    let fetch_calls = executor.fetch_calls.lock().unwrap();\n\n    // Find a shard that has multiple fetch calls (indicating pagination)\n    let mut found_paginated_shard = None;\n    for shard_id in 0..shards {\n .iter()\n            .map(|((_, _, offset), _)| *offset)\n            .collect();\n\n        if offsets.len() > 1 {\n            found_paginated_shard = Some((shard_id, offsets));\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    assert!(\n        found_paginated_shard.is_some(),\n        \"Should have multiple fetch calls for at least one shard with {} docs (pagination needed)\",\n        docs_per_shard\n    );\n\n    let (shard_id, offsets) = found_paginated_shard.unwrap();\n    println!(\"Shard {} has paginated fetches with offsets: {:?}\", shard_id, offsets);\n\n    // Verify offsets are multiples of batch size\n    for offset in &offsets {\n        assert!(\n            *offset % 1000 == 0,\n            \"Offset {} should be a multiple of batch size 1000\",\n            offset\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Verify all documents were migrated for the paginated shard\n    let new_node_docs = executor.get_stored_doc_count(\"node-2\", shard_id);\n    assert!(\n        new_node_docs == docs_per_shard,\n        \"All {} documents should be migrated for shard {}, got {}\",\n        docs_per_shard,\n        shard_id,\n        new_node_docs\n Self(format!(\"rebalance:{}\", index_uid))\n    }\n\n    /// Get the index UID from the job ID.\n    pub fn index_uid(&self) -> &str {\n        self.0.strip_prefix(\"rebalance:\").unwrap_or(&self.0)\n phase: String,\n    /// Documents migrated so far.\n    pub docs_migrated: u64,\n    /// Last offset for pagination resume.\n    pub last_offset: u32,\n    /// Source node for migration.\n    pub source_node: Option<String>,\n    /// Target node for migration.\n    pub target_node: String,\n}\n\n/// Per-shard migration state for the worker.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, ShardMigrationPhase {\n    /// Waiting to start.\n    Idle,\n    /// Dual-write active.\n    DualWriteStarted,\n    /// Background migration in progress.\n    MigrationInProgress,\n    /// Migration complete, preparing cutover.\n    MigrationComplete,\n    /// Dual-write stopped.\n    DualWriteStopped,\n    /// Old replica deleted.\n    OldReplicaDeleted,\n    /// Migration failed.\n    Failed,\n}\n\n/// State machine for a rebalance job (per index).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\nstruct RebalanceJob {\n    /// Job ID.\n    id: RebalanceJobId,\n    /// Index UID mpsc::Sender<TopologyChangeEvent> {\n278\t        self.event_tx.clone()\n279\t    }\n280\t\n281\t    /// Start the background worker.\n282\t    ///\n283\t    /// This runs in a loop:\n284\t    /// 1. Try to acquire leader lease for each index (scope: rebalance:<index>)\n285\t    /// 2. If acquired, process events and run migrations\n286\t    /// 3. Renew lease periodically\n287\t    /// 4. If lease lost, go back to step 1\n288\t    pub async fn run(&self) {\n289\t        info!(\n290\t            pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n291\t            \"rebalancer worker starting\"\n292\t        );\n293\t\n294\t        loop {\n295\t            // Try to acquire leader lease for each index we're managing\n296\t            let mut leader_scopes = Vec::new();\n297\t\n298\t            // Get all active indexes from current jobs and use default scope\n299\t            let jobs = self.jobs.read().await;\n300\t            let mut index_uids: Vec<String> = jobs.values()\n301\t                .map(|j| j.index_uid.clone())\n302\t                .collect();\n303\t\n304\t            // Always include \"default\" scope for rebalancer operations\n305\t            index_uids.push(\"default\".to_string());\n306\t            drop(jobs);\n307\t\n308\t            // Build scopes for each index: rebalance:<index>\n309\t            let scopes: Vec<String> = index_uids\n310\t                .into_iter()\n311\t                .map(|uid| format!(\"rebalance:{}\", uid))\n312\t                .collect();\n313\t\n314\t            let mut acquired_any = false;\n315\t            for scope in &scopes {\n316\t                let now_ms = now_ms();\n317\t                let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n318\t\n319\t pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n323\t                    move || {\n324\t                        task_store.try_acquire_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at, now_ms)\n325\t                    }\n326\t                })\n327\t                .await\n328\t                {\n329\t                    Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n330\t                        info!(scope = %scope, pod_id = %self.pod_id, \"acquired leader lease\");\n331\t                        leader_scopes.push(scope.clone());\n332\t                        acquired_any = true;\n333\t                    }\n334\t                    Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n335\t                        debug!(scope = %scope, \"leader lease already held\");\n336\t                    }\n337\t                    Ok(Err(e)) => {\n338\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to acquire leader lease\");\n339\t                    }\n340\t                    Err(e) => {\n341\t                        error!(scope = %scope, error update rebalancer metrics\n348\t                {\n349\t                    let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n350\t                    metrics.start_rebalance();\n351\t                }\n352\t\n353\t                // Call metrics callback for rebalance start\n354\t                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_callback {\n355\t                    callback(true, None, None);\n356\t                }\n357\t\n358\t                // We are the leader - run the main loop\n359\t                if let Err(e) = self.run_leader_loop(&leader_scopes).await {\n360\t                    error!(error = %e, \"leader loop failed\");\n361\t                }\n362\t\n363\t                // Clear rebalancer in-progress status on exit\n364\t                {\n365\t                    let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n366\t                    metrics.end_rebalance();\n367\t                }\n368\t\n369\t                // Call metrics callback for rebalance end\n370\t                if let Some(ref callback) = self.metrics_callback {\n371\t                    callback(false, None, None);\n372\t                }\n373\t            } else {\n374\t                // Not the leader - wait before retrying\n375\t                tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(\n376\t                    self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n377\t                ))\n378\t                .await;\n379\t            }\n380\t        }\n381\t    }\n382\t\n383\t    /// Run the leader loop: process events, renew lease, drive migrations.\n384\t    async fn run_leader_loop(&self, scopes: &[String]) -> Result<(), String> {\n385\t        let mut lease_renewal = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n386\t            self.config.lease_renewal_interval_ms,\n387\t        ));\n388\t\n389\t        // Take the receiver out of the Option\n390\t        let mut event_rx = {\n391\t            let mut rx_guard = self.event_rx.write().await;\n392\t            rx_guard.take().ok_or_else(|| \"event receiver already taken\".to_string())?\n393\t        };\n394\t\n395\t        let result = async {\n396\t            loop {\n397\t                tokio::select! {\n398\t                    // Renew lease periodically\n399\t                    _ = lease_renewal.tick() => {\n400\t                        for scope in scopes {\n401\t                            let now_ms = now_ms();\n402\t                            let expires_at = now_ms + (self.config.lease_ttl_secs * 1000) as i64;\n403\t\n404\t                             let pod_id = self.pod_id.clone();\n408\t                                move || {\n409\t                                    task_store.renew_leader_lease(&scope, &pod_id, expires_at)\n410\t                                }\n411\t                            })\n412\t                            .await\n413\t                            {\n414\t                                Ok(Ok(true)) => {\n415\t                                    debug!(scope = %scope, \"renewed leader lease\");\n416\t                                }\n417\t                                Ok(Ok(false)) => {\n418\t                                    info!(scope = %scope, \"lost leader lease\");\n419\t                                    return Ok::<(), String>(()); // Exit loop, will retry acquisition\n420\t                                }\n421\t                                Ok(Err(e)) => {\n422\t                                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"failed to renew lease\");\n423\t                                    return Err(format!(\"lease renewal failed: {}\", e));\n424\t                                }\n425\t                                Err(e) => {\n426\t                                    error!(scope = %scope, error = %e, \"spawn_blocking task failed\");\n427\t                                    return Err(format!(\"lease renewal task      error!(error = %e, \"failed to handle topology event\");\n437\t                        }\n438\t                    }\n439\t\n440\t                    // Drive active migrations\n441\t                    _ = tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)) => {\n442\t                        if let Err(e) = self.drive_migrations().await {\n443\t                            error!(error = %e, \"failed to drive migrations\");\n444\t                        }\n445\t                    }\n446\t                }\n447\t            }\n448\t        }.await;\n449\t\n450\t        // Put the receiver
